name: The Texas Flyover
subject: Pecan Is King, Bucc-ee's Sues Duckees, and Avoiding 'Brown Friday'
body:
Pumpkin pie is the iconic Thanksgiving dessert, but another pie is more popular in Texas and several other Southern states. You guessed it: pecan pie.

| | | [ ](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0zztu6zAQheHVkJ0FzgwlkgWLe2Goc9qUAh8jiwAVGRLj2LsPnDqpz8EH_O-X6RJKLR_Xt7Cyb495m1ChPgGc0E45lPqctMw-quxAsgdDaBz1WsnFRxudMuAwoLOMEIE4ppl6ToOhmGTxLwwALQApcB1AcBZ7hUNOWlEvtOJc2rZ3beHGj3DM9bndee_Stsrql9Zuh6B_AkeB4y-f10DjZ1sFnc__5e7z7avUWo7BCK2uayj1h2p_tt09fgcAAP__APFP5A)
---
| | |
# Thursday, November 28th, 2024
---|---
**Happy Thanksgiving!** On this day in 1884, a home for impoverished and disabled Confederate veterans received a state charter. The [Texas Confederate Home](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0z7GSqyAUgOGngU6HcwSVguI2drntlg6RozCLkAEmJm-_s-n3Bb5__q_berMhhnT8tyeZ9trzigJlB9DhvDob4nuV3Jm7cBo4GZgGnPSgpODe7HKWSEIp0EAb4KamnaygSZJSGkcezC8GgDPAIED3AFbPqASObpNiUEwKcqHl0jdPjV627vGdn1T6LZ88Gt_ao7LhH8OF4XJdV9-qtznFkKjP5WC4eJvcPedvhgulVgJVhsuH6racdnJUbKPO55N4Me5xhRhDHScmxXHaED-l9uf60-BPAAAA__-bLlvN) in Austin housed over 2,000 veterans from its opening in 1886 to the death of the last veteran in 1954. We hope you enjoy a wonderful Thanksgiving with your friends and family. If you're having pecan pie, you're [in good company](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kDGPtCAUAH-Ndhh44CIFxddst197pXkrTyWLQoST9d9f9vrrJzPJfD3GB_rg9-U_bmTLe44jcFBMCAbD6NCHa1Sts0_ujGjJCi1BG9kr3q7WoRpmxKfRmtR80zOqaaIeuFE910613n5kQsAghOTCdEKgGaDncHOT4rJvFCfnSzy6slKhN-Y5XPGko5vi1ga7lpJyI_81cG_gXmvtdqq5Er0-QAP3DRPLa6yZbTEXlmL6DniwsuL-yos__b6w5InRScfFcsFCTJhBGqnbw7pUfQg-33Sj-LKhD7_d8ueI08JPAAAA__-jdWFJ). Let us know if it's your favorite Thanksgiving dessert in today's poll! We work hard to bring you _The Flyover_ every day without the legacy media bias. If you appreciate our work, please read a special message below from our CEO, Cole.

## Paxton Leads Suit Against Money Managers
Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton is leading a coalition of 11 Republican-led states in suing BlackRock, Vanguard, and State Street, accusing them of violating antitrust laws by [influencing coal companies to reduce production](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kLGOwyAQRL8Gd1iwhtgUFNe4y7VXWsSs41UwREDs5O9PiXTlVVOM5mn0fs7T2VGgeP12G9r6XNIEAhSXksMweUfhNanG24vwRjZoZd9BbzqtRLNaM_dOeNT-ogepT6hcJ425wOKEht6LhuwbJiUMUnZCmlZKZwbQAk5-VqLTTAn0VFNu64oVn64s4ZV2zO2ctibYtdZ7Yd0Xg5HBeBxHe3u6-C4ZjBGPwmD8zP6SlwcWTnHHUjeMlS-Ut8KXlLmLlXjNj1L5Tim4SikWfs80I1_phm9Gk62_HxQClVPPlLhujsLnS_1Xzm7hNwAA__9PK2i6), leading to higher energy prices. The federal lawsuit alleges that the country’s three largest money managers used their investments and participation in environmental initiatives to pressure coal companies to [reduce output by more than half by 2030](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kDFvgzAQhX-N2RzZZwfD4KELW7p2RAe-gBsDkX2Q5N9Xqbp2fe_p6dP3dekvGFNcp09cyPPzuvWgwEqtJTR9wJheva2CH1RodUVeOwOuNWerqtlTg9a2rXPK1c3gRmNrqI1ujUNXwzBW0b_PtIZGa6N0e9Ia2wbOCuowWmXOwioKkbd84pmYnliu6bUdlE_jtlTJz8z3IsyHgE5A970X5plWepR3L6Cb3tt1oZUFdOO2Zy4y4UNAp7UsjEwSpyLLTnJION7yNt7-8sKZiOWB67RjDhJToolCeskjbgk5rlOVfbg_Ykqx1E5YNS0Y0y8Z_6vq8PATAAD__5b4bYo). The states are asking the court to bar the firms from continuing market manipulation. “ _Texas will not tolerate the illegal weaponization of the financial industry in service of a destructive, politicized environmental agenda_ ,” [Paxton said](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx00L2OqzAUBOCngc7IPxBM4eI2dLntlujEPhArxkb2CYS3XyXaLbeb4tNoNF_X6Qo--Lj8hxUNveY0SS5bJgSTenLgwzm1tTM37gZRoxG9kv2gupbXd2N5p3Dgys44K8X7GTTXF6lnjXjTFmtv3mVCSC2E4mJohIBBy47Li7MtV13VcnSeUm7ojoQvKHM40465sWmtg7kTbaVS_yo5VnI8jqP5ICBKOeK5YMQMoVnSXskx4lEqOWYMCAXf8dexH8geGNkGL0qRlScWdgtgHznZBysEhKxQRiQG0bEd4vKE7JgPARcI4WQ2xbL57OPCVoh-ewYgrLNx2-FD8OXSVy1fVvDhM5_-_HM38jsAAP__HDx-Jg).
## Study: Organized Retail Theft on the Rise
A [newly released study](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0UL1yszAQfBqpg9EdkkGFiq-h89emZIR0GE2E5REXYr99BhfpUu7P7O7sx3W6-pTT_fbfb-T4uZQJFeoGoMFhij7l16RldLOKFiQ56DvsbWe0kqvDwUdlZj2b3viwLACRDMAQwFxsUFYmd4YB4ADQKbAtgLcDGoWXGLTqjNCKYuJSW16J6en3Jb_KQbUNZZPZrcyPXXT_BI4Cx1C2B9eSM9X2bW5v5RA4-rl8scBxo5h8E-jOVH_hZzqlc4fAsRL7lBteaTlZWV18fKec037phVa3zaf8ruY_vzgc_gQAAP__wu9gIA) from the Texas Comptroller’s office highlights the rise of organized retail theft in the state, which is estimated to exceed $463 million in lost sales and tax revenue every year. A recently created task force has recommended [creating a statewide reporting system](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx00LGOqzAQheGnsTsjezAEFy5uQ5fbbolm8QRGciCyR5Ds06-Sfvuj70j_13W6Imfelv94pyjP2z6BBW-cMzBMCTm_Jq9T_LYpOE3RXVq4hLbzVq8RCQGc7_oQbD947Mi3qUfwFnvo5qA5vjHnYHCutS40zmEYoLPQp9nbtlPeUmLZSyMrCT2x3vJrP6g0837XOa4ij6rafwpGBeN5ns2BOdNrpk0K5vdKwbjRWRWMhR57EVPoIMzVFK68LWYvC278Q8kUEuRsZKWbGN7M50_BqEtMj5Nz5tpflLfLHflDa_mzyBHhNwAA__9xXGRO) to share crime data and track offenders across cities, enabling better coordination among law enforcement. Proposed changes include revising laws requiring stores to wait 10 days to report thefts and implementing store walk-through programs to identify vulnerabilities. “ _Are we going to completely eliminate it tomorrow? No. Are we going to significantly reduce it? Yes_ ,” State Comptroller Glenn Hegar said.
## Pecan Pie Is King In Texas
Pumpkin pie is the iconic Thanksgiving dessert, but [another pie is more popular](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kDGPtCAUAH-Ndhh44CIFxddst197pXkrTyWLQoST9d9f9vrrJzPJfD3GB_rg9-U_bmTLe44jcFBMCAbD6NCHa1Sts0_ujGjJCi1BG9kr3q7WoRpmxKfRmtR80zOqaaIeuFE910613n5kQsAghOTCdEKgGaDncHOT4rJvFCfnSzy6slKhN-Y5XPGko5vi1ga7lpJyI_81cG_gXmvtdqq5Er0-QAP3DRPLa6yZbTEXlmL6DniwsuL-yos__b6w5InRScfFcsFCTJhBGqnbw7pUfQg-33Sj-LKhD7_d8ueI08JPAAAA__-jdWFJ) in Texas and several other Southern states. You guessed it: pecan pie. According to a [2018 survey by GE Appliances](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0j7FugzAQQL_GbCDf4WAYPHRhS9eO6GIf4SQTI9tKwt9X6d796T29n-tyJYnyuH_Tzq6-17SgRtMCtDgugSSei2mCu-kwQcMObI926i9GN5vTGoYwDpastn4i8IFXMAEnby3eLDTiPjIAHAF6DVMHQNOIF41D8Eb3F2U0B6kpd3Xjym8qazzTk3Pn095Et9V6FNV_KZwVzkfmUnJKe3dnOo4o9PBcPqjC-cGvonCmnbN4aku70jNlqdxuKUqgsz2ES5NdOF4So5TBKqPvO0n8i9V_758OfwMAAP__9PVdVA), pecan pie is the dessert of choice in Texas, Arkansas, Louisiana, and Oklahoma. Apart from folks in the Northeast, who prefer apple pie after their Thanksgiving meal, most of the rest of the country goes for pumpkin pie. Texas is one of the country’s leading producers of pecans, with an output of [31 million pounds in 2023](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kMGOokAQhp-muEGqqxuBQx9YlawJutnoZjd7IS000BkUQregl3n2iRiPc6kv9X9JJfX_3Rd7ZTpzbQ7qoqW7131BSMJnzKe4qJTpHoXwKnnGKmGeliziFCU8FOi1kvFVHak4ZjFTdV1rDMWZ0bnGkEel1ugZ-TzGGMWMcWRJwJhKYgqRVlUpkIcgUFfG9WPgWu30Xdm6e_STHoOyv3idbJ0bLPAUKAPK5nkOrsra4GYrFTT9BJT9vpnyozg65SxQljbFr0mPk9EzUGadcvq9B0M7AH9lwDen7b_0CMSBp5_AU6fvDvjmz2mX7_5vN0AhrZ8Dd4cFx5_bPPeB1hwXF-OL-CLmP4DWvoiWkCO9pTfKaphN1xm7ikBgc1GmW55z37Y9SfoKAAD__wTEd3k). Extreme weather in recent years has hampered production, which has reached as high as [60-70 million pounds](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kLtuhTAQRL_GdIvsNc_CRRq6mzYlMrBgK4Ate-_r7yNun2Y0xehodH5u48363Z_btz3I8GsNI0qsQCnAblys399jVSxmkkuvCjKq1dj2uq5k4UxjbT_bTulG0oR9v3ZEU9u1uqrrFbEtvLlgSmGnlJaqL5WyfYe1xGaZK6lrUUlaPIdUsiOml83r_g4PSuUcjmI3jjlmob8EDgKHNSR-hsQuUQyJy5A2gQNK1AIHpa5-hecM550zfIAQabYnrDYdlDKw_SVwnmFN4YAlhfvmGKY7Q3YhxmvyDCdDJoLZ2XOjDP6EmPxMAocimSU-_b773LSiktth_f45y__aexj8CwAA__-mW3Kl) in the past.
| **Dear Texas Flyover Reader,** Happy Thanksgiving! I hope your day is filled with family, food, and football—and I hope you take a little time to consider the blessings we enjoy in this great land. Today, I’m thankful for turkey (the white meat, of course), my two children (even when they act like turkeys; ever had a bow and arrow fired at your head during a Zoom call?), and for the freedom to enjoy it all. Here’s another one of mine: The demand in America for biased, elitist news media is dropping. MSNBC’s audience is down 39% since October, and Comcast has announced plans to spin the network off into a new company. (The new entity will be named...SpinCo. These things really do write themselves. ) It’s a bright sign for America’s future that folks are ditching the propaganda factories, and I want as many of those people as possible to join our movement for reliable news. Here’s another thing I’m thankful for: Over 250,000 of you have already joined _The Texas Flyover ,_ and you’ve been telling your friends about us! I’m grateful for each of you. Today, if _The Texas Flyover_ is on your list of things to be thankful for, would you help me with a donation? Your direct support is the best way to help us cover our costs, and it would mean a lot to me. clean_body: Pumpkin pie is the iconic Thanksgiving dessert, but another pie is more popular in Texas and several other Southern states. You guessed it: pecan pie. | | | | | | Thursday, November 28th, 2024 ---|--- Happy Thanksgiving! On this day in 1884, a home for impoverished and disabled Confederate veterans received a state charter. The in Austin housed over 2,000 veterans from its opening in 1886 to the death of the last veteran in 1954. We hope you enjoy a wonderful Thanksgiving with your friends and family. If you're having pecan pie, you're . Let us know if it's your favorite Thanksgiving dessert in today's poll! We work hard to bring you The Flyover every day without the legacy media bias. If you appreciate our work, please read a special message below from our CEO, Cole. Abilene ☁️ 51/38° | Amarillo 🌥️ 47/33° | Austin ☁️ 62/46° | Brownsville ☁️ 83/64° | Corpus Christi 🌦️ 73/59° | DFW ☁️ 54/42° | El Paso ☁️ 67/48° | Houston ☁️ 72/53° | Lubbock ☁️ 49/36° | San Antonio ☁️ 66/50° | Texarkana 🌤️ 58/40° | Paxton Leads Suit Against Money Managers Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton is leading a coalition of 11 Republican-led states in suing BlackRock, Vanguard, and State Street, accusing them of violating antitrust laws by , leading to higher energy prices. The federal lawsuit alleges that the country’s three largest money managers used their investments and participation in environmental initiatives to pressure coal companies to . The states are asking the court to bar the firms from continuing market manipulation. “ Texas will not tolerate the illegal weaponization of the financial industry in service of a destructive, politicized environmental agenda ,” . Study: Organized Retail Theft on the Rise A from the Texas Comptroller’s office highlights the rise of organized retail theft in the state, which is estimated to exceed $463 million in lost sales and tax revenue every year. A recently created task force has recommended to share crime data and track offenders across cities, enabling better coordination among law enforcement. Proposed changes include revising laws requiring stores to wait 10 days to report thefts and implementing store walk-through programs to identify vulnerabilities. “ Are we going to completely eliminate it tomorrow? No. Are we going to significantly reduce it? Yes ,” State Comptroller Glenn Hegar said. Pecan Pie Is King In Texas Pumpkin pie is the iconic Thanksgiving dessert, but in Texas and several other Southern states. You guessed it: pecan pie. According to a , pecan pie is the dessert of choice in Texas, Arkansas, Louisiana, and Oklahoma. Apart from folks in the Northeast, who prefer apple pie after their Thanksgiving meal, most of the rest of the country goes for pumpkin pie. Texas is one of the country’s leading producers of pecans, with an output of . Extreme weather in recent years has hampered production, which has reached as high as in the past. | Dear Texas Flyover Reader, Happy Thanksgiving! I hope your day is filled with family, food, and football—and I hope you take a little time to consider the blessings we enjoy in this great land. Today, I’m thankful for turkey (the white meat, of course), my two children (even when they act like turkeys; ever had a bow and arrow fired at your head during a Zoom call?), and for the freedom to enjoy it all. Here’s another one of mine: The demand in America for biased, elitist news media is dropping. MSNBC’s audience is down 39% since October, and Comcast has announced plans to spin the network off into a new company. (The new entity will be named...SpinCo. These things really do write themselves. ) It’s a bright sign for America’s future that folks are ditching the propaganda factories, and I want as many of those people as possible to join our movement for reliable news. Here’s another thing I’m thankful for: Over 250,000 of you have already joined The Texas Flyover , and you’ve been telling your friends about us! I’m grateful for each of you. Today, if The Texas Flyover is on your list of things to be thankful for, would you help me with a donation? Your direct support is the best way to help us cover our costs, and it would mean a lot to me. Whether or not you can donate, I’m thankful for your support! I hope you have a fantastic Thanksgiving. -Cole, CEO | ➤ Mark Swidan , a former Houston resident jailed in China for 12 years on a drug charge, was released and is returning to the United States with two other American detainees. () ➤ Starr County: Texas General Land Commissioner Dawn Buckingham visited the site of a planned 1.5-mile border wall on a portion of the state’s newly acquired 1,402-acre ranch. Buckingham has offered the site to the incoming Trump administration as a staging area for deportations. () ➤ Lubbock: Xcel Energy will begin using AI-equipped cameras to detect wildfires and alert first responders. The Smokehouse Creek Fire, which burned more than a million acres in the Panhandle earlier this year, was caused by power lines that fell when a utility pole broke. () ➤ Austin: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton has asked a judge to shut down the Sunrise Homeless Navigation Center, alleging it is a “magnet” for drug and criminal activity. () ➤ White Settlement: Police detained a man found with three firearms and two explosive devices in a van early Tuesday morning. Although the suspect’s motives are unknown, a neighbor said, “ If you have that kind of stuff on you, you’re planning something. ” () ➤ College Station: An apartment complex built years ago has been reclassified as student housing, meaning residents—which include business professionals, families, and elderly residents—must go to the post office to retrieve their mail. name: The Flyover
subject: Trump Cabinet Bomb Threats, Ancient Sandwiches, and a Popsicle Caper
body:
Several of President-elect Donald Trump's Cabinet nominees and administration appointees faced [bomb threats and "swatting" attacks](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx00DGvnDAQBOBfA50RuwZsChdp6C5tSmTwAr4YG9mbQ_fvo3uvfuVopNGn-fOYH9YHH_ff9iTDW5qxxU4ACNSzsz685652ZiNCkjUZUBLVKJUe68P0SrYKUEmnx02DA7BuHKBb1570skHtzWcNADWAHLBvuqUbeuzcMoBW46iqriXnOeXmmXzkg7bwTi_KzZrOOpiD-SqV_FXhVOF033cTlzXSXT59hdOVgme_lgonl6INTnD-d14VToVelG34zuLy698iOF3imZYi2OadmJxY0rmIcltmH3fBRybLReQ1WtDYSqizcdftQ_Bl-Fj30_rwZeOfvnoZ_B8AAP__I_ttPg) on Tuesday and Wednesday.

| | |
---
| | |
# Thursday, November 28, 2024
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1520, Portuguese navigator [Ferdinand Magellan](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0z7GOhCAQxvGn0Q6zDCJQUFxjt9deaWZlWOaCslGyxre_eLn26n_y_fJ93ac7cub1-YkL-RrLBDfohZQC7BSQ8zn1bfCRCEi15KVRYJwy1rXJS4wmONQqPjSR1VHqWbqoEG3QIYSW_bUmJVgp1QC66x_9oKEPj0Fa45xp-hsFrmXrvguvNVHMZ3nT1s1labNPtb72Rn00MDYwHsfRJd5r2c6rNzDWxLsIeApexV9pYFzwSTnjKjbCOdEuaiLxwpkjz-3mw-vgnHkfLv25IOdfrf73_u3hJwAA__-fX13o), leading three ships, entered a new ocean, naming it the Pacific for its calm waters. To our readers: Everyone at _The Flyover_ is grateful for your support and wishes you a wonderful Thanksgiving! We work hard to bring you _The Flyover_ every day without the legacy media bias. If you appreciate our work, please read a special message below from our CEO, Cole.
|
---
## Trump Cabinet Picks Get Bomb Threats
Several of President-elect Donald Trump's Cabinet nominees and administration appointees faced [bomb threats and "swatting" attacks](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx00DGvnDAQBOBfA50RuwZsChdp6C5tSmTwAr4YG9mbQ_fvo3uvfuVopNGn-fOYH9YHH_ff9iTDW5qxxU4ACNSzsz685652ZiNCkjUZUBLVKJUe68P0SrYKUEmnx02DA7BuHKBb1570skHtzWcNADWAHLBvuqUbeuzcMoBW46iqriXnOeXmmXzkg7bwTi_KzZrOOpiD-SqV_FXhVOF033cTlzXSXT59hdOVgme_lgonl6INTnD-d14VToVelG34zuLy698iOF3imZYi2OadmJxY0rmIcltmH3fBRybLReQ1WtDYSqizcdftQ_Bl-Fj30_rwZeOfvnoZ_B8AAP__I_ttPg) on Tuesday and Wednesday. Rep. Elise Stefanik, Trump's nominee for U.N. ambassador, was among those targeted while traveling with her family, while former Rep. Matt Gaetz, who recently withdrew his bid for attorney general, had a bomb squad respond to his Florida home. The FBI is investigating the numerous threats targeting appointees for departments including Agriculture, Housing, and Labor, with some receiving [pro-Palestinian-themed messages](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0z72OgzAQBOCnsbsgvGtsKFxcQ5drr0T-WRKDwRFYsfL2J66_bqSRvtH83Ke7jSnuj2-7kSlznqAFeRPiBv0UbEyfSfJgZiIg5GSERtAD6n7gT6NaAic6b52cUSsUWg0BtfKE1HZO82guTQjohUAFXSOdVB3I4JTo9TBoJlsKseSjWXLcy5Pm9MlvOhqfN57Ms5TXyfCLwchgrLU2zvmrYzDuVE8Goz1K9Imu6Be1LtjjKtfMDxNeNaYUT3WtPDYb059a_nv5NvAbAAD__6rpUls) and others having bomb squads dispatched to their homes. None of the incidents involved Trump or Vice President-elect JD Vance, who are under Secret Service protection, while transition spokeswoman Karoline Leavitt condemned the threats as "[violent, unAmerican threats](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kLFutDAQhJ_GdEa3ax_GhYu_obu_TYnM2YZNDD7hzZHL00ekTzfSSN-MvrfbePOUaZv_-zU6TmXEC2oJILEfg6f8GnUTXIoRo2qiA6PQWGV62ywueOh62wWNoFLw6npPgCbY0E8J7B0acicNAHsA1eG11ZPurqjD1EFvrDVCX2IgLnv7XmjjJab8Ks-4t_eyNtktzI8q1D-Bg8DhOI7Wf1GpZytwONkCB4AzG4ED75_rQ05lnSQve_RcZT08M22zrByT3-hDzj7yd7O78DgoZ6rdeWJePeXfUf5LwtPhTwAAAP__m8Zdfw)."
## China Releases Three American Prisoners
The Biden administration secured [the release of three Americans](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0zzFvhSAUBeBfA9szckGRgaGL2-va0YBc3qO5ilUi8d83du92kpN8J-frOT1dorS-Pt2CtsQ8QQvqIcQDhim4RNekeLAREVBytEJL0EbqwfC3Deh1Bw5bhcLLFo2OEb0zQ2wRjO94srcmBAxCyB66RnnVd6CC78WgjdFMtRhSyXvzndNa3hjpyifuzZwXTvZdynYw-cFgZDDWWhvv57tjMK5YDwaj20uaCe840wW06e78UZnvNmw1EaWjv1dei0v0p5b_Xp4WfgMAAP__e71TrA) detained in China through a prisoner swap involving at least one Chinese citizen held in U.S. custody. U.S. officials confirmed [Mark Swidan, Kai Li, and John Leung](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kLGO6yAURL8Gd7bgmoApKF5Dl9duaV2H65gVNhGQWP77FdtvN8XRzNF83ec7hhiO53_cydY1zcBB9kL0MM0eQ7xm2Xm7EgGNHVmhR9Bm1JPpNivNunh5A26ERFyQr0Z6ArVwbkapxi7Y1iYETEKMCm6DXKS6gfSLEpM2RjPJyYea8vCdwlE3WuOVPpSHR9q7aLdaX4WN_xg4Bu48z-FdsCaPVwMYuFJTvhi4g87SiJSjZ-DaKgMnRMuagXuX_rGFA3vcKYcHHqXPFAkLMXBaKaG01pw3tMvWv84QYyiq-T13DPHXp_71z8fCTwAAAP__Fx9jQw) were freed following years of imprisonment on alleged spying and drug-trafficking charges. The U.S. had classified Li and Swidan as [wrongfully detained](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx00LGOtCAUBeCngc6JXECxoPgbu_nbLQ3KZbgbBINkzLz9xu23PjlfTs7Xc3k6SpRf_92OtoWyQA-qE6IDs3hH6bMo7m1ABJQcrRgljJMczcSjxVU4IzHoPqwGVNjWsAm3hUFrOaBXnOytCQFGCDmAfqhVDRqUXwdhxmkamerRUyv18V0ot4ghfcob62MrO082tnacTP5jMDOYW8RIKd0ZgzmWHTNeJ4PZ-Z0yna26RiUzmHUvxGR0t0XKrjsqnSVj7c7LHZ3bsdLm8l3k1frjopToHO4tr93Rr8_bX1-8LfwEAAD__4eYYZM), engaging in numerous talks with Chinese officials over the past four years regarding a possible release. The White House [has not disclosed who](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kLGOgzAQRL8GOqPs2mBTuLiGLtdeiRZ7CT4ZOzJWUP7-xPXpRnrSzOj93Oc7hRjS45t2tnXNM95QCQCBZvYU4ntWrbcrM7Js2YKWqEepzdhuVsreaWIwQCgR1tWg17rv3YJaeb-2wV5tAGgA5IB9pxY19Kj8MoDR46gbdWMfai7dbw6pbrzGd35x6Vze22i3Wp9HI78anBqczvPs0uISn8fFG5yueIFcom9wkoJ2LsFROoTnSiGxF24LiQQVFoUj08FeFJcIDIyjbIv1zzPEGI7hOvPYKcT_8fpJxsviXwAAAP__B9pgyg), if anyone, was released in exchange for the three U.S. citizens.
## How America Celebrates Thanksgiving
Americans are regionally divided on trends, family traditions, and what makes a city a festive destination, according to studies on how the nation celebrates Thanksgiving. One study found that [Americans even pronounce Thanksgiving](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kE2r3CAYhX-N2Tnoq1GzcDF0CN3cQmmhy8GP18Q2MSF6J8y_LzP7uzoHHjjwnD8f9w-Xl1ymH25F29J2BwaSck7B3KPLy_Muu2gTIqDo0HItQA9Cm6GbrQ9K-J7JlKQEzZnmTAVnhgQDiym6LtvXGudgOBcK-ov0UvUgo1fc6GHQRDKMuW3H5e-WS5sxLc_tgcclbGu32Lm1vRJxJTASGM_zvKy1vBiBEQv9rATGgucr3n11O63zdlYac0p4YGn0dM9Ka3MNK63uSdvsyr865UcuE4HRHfR65Z8_b04TMW4hRyJua8U4lbYTUHsg4vb92-9fBFR4vGmPCYxMPgYlZWLeB2DeIPbOpOh0IqAwE3Hj0B027mdellzVy3VaXV7ebu2rrx8W_gcAAP__fyZ-Iw) in three distinct ways: “THANGS-giv-ing,” “THANKSgiving,” and “thanksGIVing,” reflecting regional differences in speech. Thanksgiving [side dishes](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kDFvgzAQRn8NbEbc2bHN4KFLtnTtiJz4AtcYjLCLlX9f0b37p-89va_bePMceZ0-_UKuPNOIPSoBINCOwXN8j6oN7kmEJFtyYCSaQRo7tLMDi4jaWO_7gBqkApCPh7Q2gDGX3rbszjcAtABS46VTd6UvqMJdgzXDYBrVU-CS9u478VpmesZ3OmjvHmlpo5tL2XIjPxq8NnittXYr1VyJXuegweuSchFb2n6i30WZ_frKEx-8TiJzIBE4z4IO2t8iF19IwGCl1qrdXdgqx8hZnw7T4jn-Mct_DQ6HvwEAAP__EQxdgw) also had regional preferences, with candied yams favored in the West, green bean casserole in the Midwest, cornbread dressing in the South, and potato casserole in the East. A recent survey named San Diego, California, as [the best city to visit for Thanksgiving](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0z7GOqzAQheGngc6IGRtsFy5uky633RKZeIDZGBzFFlbefsX22x-dT__Xfbp7jnys__1Orixpwh6VABBopuA5fibVBrcQIcmWHGiJ2kptbLs5uYy6D6ZfbJg9GJDK-wdaHWY7h0Vjy-56A0ADIEccOjWrcUAV5hGMtlY3qqfAJb2778RH2WiJn3TSu3ukvY1uK-WVG_mvwVuDt1prd1DNleh5DRq87f4l8pZqFjPlIh5cmLI4OXMRZfPHM6988rEKsGYYYWjfLrwqx8h5vPB19xx_sfJX_OnwJwAA__-m51xR), citing its numerous highly rated restaurants. Atlanta, Georgia, also made the list for its holiday decorations and airport.

| Flying together with our sponsor **Dear Flyover Reader,** Happy Thanksgiving! I hope your day is filled with family, food, and football—and I hope you take a little time to consider the blessings we enjoy in this great land. Today, I’m thankful for turkey (the white meat, of course), for my two children (even when they act like turkeys; ever had a bow and arrow fired at your head during a Zoom call?), and for the freedom to enjoy it all. Here’s another one of mine: The demand in America for biased, elitist news media is dropping. MSNBC’s audience is down 39% since October, and Comcast has announced plans to spin the network off into a new company. (The new entity will be named...SpinCo. These things really do write themselves. ) It’s a bright sign for America’s future that folks are ditching the propaganda factories, and I want as many of those people as possible to join our movement for reliable news. Here’s another thing I’m thankful for: nearly a million of you have already joined _The Flyover,_ and you’ve been telling your friends about us! I’m grateful for each of you. Today, if _The Flyover_ is on your list of things to be thankful for, would you help me with a donation? Your direct support is the best way to help us cover our costs, and it would mean a lot to me. clean_body: Several of President-elect Donald Trump's Cabinet nominees and administration appointees faced on Tuesday and Wednesday. | | | | | | Thursday, November 28, 2024 ---|--- Good Morning! On this day in 1520, Portuguese navigator , leading three ships, entered a new ocean, naming it the Pacific for its calm waters. To our readers: Everyone at The Flyover is grateful for your support and wishes you a wonderful Thanksgiving! We work hard to bring you The Flyover every day without the legacy media bias. If you appreciate our work, please read a special message below from our CEO, Cole. | Trump Cabinet Picks Get Bomb Threats Several of President-elect Donald Trump's Cabinet nominees and administration appointees faced on Tuesday and Wednesday. Rep. Elise Stefanik, Trump's nominee for U.N. ambassador, was among those targeted while traveling with her family, while former Rep. Matt Gaetz, who recently withdrew his bid for attorney general, had a bomb squad respond to his Florida home. The FBI is investigating the numerous threats targeting appointees for departments including Agriculture, Housing, and Labor, with some receiving and others having bomb squads dispatched to their homes. None of the incidents involved Trump or Vice President-elect JD Vance, who are under Secret Service protection, while transition spokeswoman Karoline Leavitt condemned the threats as "." China Releases Three American Prisoners The Biden administration secured detained in China through a prisoner swap involving at least one Chinese citizen held in U.S. custody. U.S. officials confirmed were freed following years of imprisonment on alleged spying and drug-trafficking charges. The U.S. had classified Li and Swidan as , engaging in numerous talks with Chinese officials over the past four years regarding a possible release. The White House , if anyone, was released in exchange for the three U.S. citizens. How America Celebrates Thanksgiving Americans are regionally divided on trends, family traditions, and what makes a city a festive destination, according to studies on how the nation celebrates Thanksgiving. One study found that in three distinct ways: “THANGS-giv-ing,” “THANKSgiving,” and “thanksGIVing,” reflecting regional differences in speech. Thanksgiving also had regional preferences, with candied yams favored in the West, green bean casserole in the Midwest, cornbread dressing in the South, and potato casserole in the East. A recent survey named San Diego, California, as , citing its numerous highly rated restaurants. Atlanta, Georgia, also made the list for its holiday decorations and airport. | ➤ President-elect Donald Trump has nominated Dr. Jay Bhattacharya, a Stanford University health policy professor known for opposing COVID-19 lockdowns, as director of the National Institutes of Health. I hope your day is filled with family, food, and football—and I hope you take a little time to consider the blessings we enjoy in this great land. Today, I’m thankful for turkey (the white meat, of course), for my two children (even when they act like turkeys; ever had a bow and arrow fired at your head during a Zoom call?), and for the freedom to enjoy it all. Here’s another one of mine: The demand in America for biased, elitist news media is dropping. MSNBC’s audience is down 39% since October, and Comcast has announced plans to spin the network off into a new company. (The new entity will be named...SpinCo. These things really do write themselves. ) It’s a bright sign for America’s future that folks are ditching the propaganda factories, and I want as many of those people as possible to join our movement for reliable news. Here’s another thing I’m thankful for: nearly a million of you have already joined The Flyover, and you’ve been telling your friends about us! I’m grateful for each of you. name: Hillsdale College via Conservative Broadcast
subject: Before they're gone
body:
Only a fraction of the 16 million Americans who fought bravely in World War II remain with us.

This sponsored message from Conservative Broadcast is on behalf of Hillsdale College
[](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/3MIRsflmxvCmkwzbGgBIVw~~/AARclAA~) Dear Fellow American,
Only a fraction of the 16 million Americans who fought bravely in World War II remain with us. This fact means that we’re losing more and more living memory of the greatest armed conflict in human history, which makes the study of World War II even more important.
To this end, Hillsdale College has produced an online course called **“The Second World Wars.”**
**Taught by historian Victor Davis Hanson** , this course explores how the conflict’s diverse theaters, belligerents, and ways of fighting came eventually to define a single war. And it examines the key investments, strategies, and leaders that led the Allied powers to victory over the Axis. [](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/3MIRsflmxvCmkwzbGgBIVw~~/AARclAA~) If you’d like to secure a DVD set before they’re all gone, you can get one shipped directly to your home for any gift of $100 or more to Hillsdale College.
Here’s a secure link to reserve your copy of **“The Second World Wars”** on DVD:
**[https://secured.hillsdale.edu/hillsdale/support-the-second-world-wars](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/3MIRsflmxvCmkwzbGgBIVw~~/AARclAA~)**
Thank you for preserving liberty for future generations of Americans through education with Hillsdale College!
Best regards,
Jeremiah Regan, Ph.D. Executive Director of Online Learning Hillsdale College
P.S. The **“The Second World Wars”** DVD set is great as a homeschool curriculum or for independent study. Plus, it makes a perfect gift for anyone who wants to know more about the Second World War. **[Reserve your copy now.](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/3MIRsflmxvCmkwzbGgBIVw~~/AARclAA~)**

name: OKGOP
subject: Happy Thanksgiving Day!
body:
Give Thanks to God Almighty

| [ ](https://okgop.nationbuilder.com/r?u=urG6o0YzHACIJY0G92IOaIOiUFGhm1XQHaiZ59fvCoY&e=48f9f7a040435275f8f9fa993b0a5712&utm_source=okgop&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=2_happy_thanksgiving_day&n=1)
---
Peter -- As Thanksgiving approaches, we are reminded of the deep tradition of gratitude that has shaped our nation's history and culture. This special day offers an opportunity to pause, reflect, and give thanks for the many blessings in our lives. From its earliest roots in the struggles and triumphs of the first settlers to the warm family gatherings of today, Thanksgiving remains a time to honor resilience, unity, and faith. The origins of Thanksgiving are woven into the fabric of our nation’s story. In 1607, settlers in what is now Maine held a service of thanks after surviving the perilous journey across the Atlantic. Their gratitude for safe passage marked one of the first acts of Thanksgiving on American soil. A few years later, in 1621, the Pilgrims of Plymouth Colony celebrated what we now recognize as the “First Thanksgiving.” After enduring a harrowing voyage aboard the Mayflower and a brutal winter that claimed the lives of nearly half their community, the Pilgrims were aided by their Native American allies, particularly a man named Squanto. Squanto, a member of the Patuxet tribe, became a guide and a vital lifeline for the settlers. He taught them how to cultivate crops like corn, fish in local rivers, and live off the land. By November of 1621, the Pilgrims had achieved a bountiful harvest, prompting Governor William Bradford to organize a celebratory feast. Joined by their Native allies, the Pilgrims shared food, fellowship, and gratitude. This historic gathering laid the foundation for the tradition we uphold today. Thanksgiving also holds a special place in our national consciousness because of the emphasis on gratitude expressed by our founding leaders. In 1789, President George Washington issued a proclamation designating Thursday, November 26, as a national day of thanks. His words resonate to this day: "It is the duty of all nations to acknowledge the providence of Almighty God, to obey His will, to be grateful for His benefits, and humbly to implore His protection and favor." Washington’s proclamation was more than a call to prayer; it was a call to unity. It encouraged Americans to reflect on the freedoms and opportunities that have been granted to us, as well as the sacrifices made to secure them. Today, we carry forward this legacy of thanksgiving, recognizing the blessings of liberty, the abundance of peace, and the strength of our communities. Thanksgiving is not just a day of feasting—it is a time to cherish what matters most. It is a moment to honor the sacrifices of those who came before us and to express our gratitude for the people who enrich our lives today. We give thanks for the love of family and friends, the support of our neighbors, and the courage of our veterans who protect our freedoms and way of life. Here in Oklahoma, Thanksgiving also reminds us of the strength of our communities. From rural towns to bustling cities, we come together to support one another, celebrate our shared values, and build a brighter future for generations to come. Whether it’s through acts of service, sharing meals, or simply spending quality time with loved ones, Oklahomans embody the spirit of gratitude and generosity that defines this holiday. Let us also remember the importance of faith during this season. The Pilgrims turned to their faith during their most challenging times, finding strength and hope in their belief in God’s providence. As we gather with our families, let us take a moment to bow our heads and thank God for His many blessings—our homes, our health, and the freedoms we enjoy. Thanksgiving is a time to look back with gratitude and forward with hope. It is a time to celebrate the ties that bind us together as a nation and as a community. While we may face challenges, the spirit of Thanksgiving reminds us of the resilience and unity that have carried us through difficult times. From all of us at the OKGOP, we wish you and your family a safe and Happy Thanksgiving. May your holiday be filled with joy, love, and gratitude. Together, let us give thanks for the privilege of living in the greatest nation in the history of mankind. God bless you, and God bless America. For the People,

Nathan Dahm
OKGOP Chairman
[OKGOP.com](https://okgop.nationbuilder.com/r?u=urG6o0YzHACIJY0G92IOaIOiUFGhm1XQHaiZ59fvCoY&e=48f9f7a040435275f8f9fa993b0a5712&utm_source=okgop&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=2_happy_thanksgiving_day&n=2)

This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail.com](mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com). To stop receiving emails, [click here](https://okgop.nationbuilder.com/unsubscribe?e=48f9f7a040435275f8f9fa993b0a5712&utm_source=okgop&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=2_happy_thanksgiving_day&n=3).
You can also keep up with OKGOP on [Twitter](https://okgop.nationbuilder.com/r?u=JcrYNQ7dhdufXPgOgJxNGHSsRONv6mnq7ly6aBOUvGVprSj7jfHNOpP70kR_1QLA&e=48f9f7a040435275f8f9fa993b0a5712&utm_source=okgop&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=2_happy_thanksgiving_day&n=4) or [Facebook](https://okgop.nationbuilder.com/r?u=UQNagEd-DqbIXa37T8ODid9mG9NGbq3z9te4_riKc6EaGmrOrRzKc2wAaVTFbzRY&e=48f9f7a040435275f8f9fa993b0a5712&utm_source=okgop&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=2_happy_thanksgiving_day&n=5).
clean_body: Give Thanks to God Almighty | Peter -- As Thanksgiving approaches, we are reminded of the deep tradition of gratitude that has shaped our nation's history and culture. This special day offers an opportunity to pause, reflect, and give thanks for the many blessings in our lives. From its earliest roots in the struggles and triumphs of the first settlers to the warm family gatherings of today, Thanksgiving remains a time to honor resilience, unity, and faith. The origins of Thanksgiving are woven into the fabric of our nation’s story. In 1607, settlers in what is now Maine held a service of thanks after surviving the perilous journey across the Atlantic. Their gratitude for safe passage marked one of the first acts of Thanksgiving on American soil. A few years later, in 1621, the Pilgrims of Plymouth Colony celebrated what we now recognize as the “First Thanksgiving.” After enduring a harrowing voyage aboard the Mayflower and a brutal winter that claimed the lives of nearly half their community, the Pilgrims were aided by their Native American allies, particularly a man named Squanto. Squanto, a member of the Patuxet tribe, became a guide and a vital lifeline for the settlers. He taught them how to cultivate crops like corn, fish in local rivers, and live off the land. By November of 1621, the Pilgrims had achieved a bountiful harvest, prompting Governor William Bradford to organize a celebratory feast. Joined by their Native allies, the Pilgrims shared food, fellowship, and gratitude. This historic gathering laid the foundation for the tradition we uphold today. Thanksgiving also holds a special place in our national consciousness because of the emphasis on gratitude expressed by our founding leaders. In 1789, President George Washington issued a proclamation designating Thursday, November 26, as a national day of thanks. His words resonate to this day: "It is the duty of all nations to acknowledge the providence of Almighty God, to obey His will, to be grateful for His benefits, and humbly to implore His protection and favor." Washington’s proclamation was more than a call to prayer; it was a call to unity. It encouraged Americans to reflect on the freedoms and opportunities that have been granted to us, as well as the sacrifices made to secure them. Today, we carry forward this legacy of thanksgiving, recognizing the blessings of liberty, the abundance of peace, and the strength of our communities. Thanksgiving is not just a day of feasting—it is a time to cherish what matters most. It is a moment to honor the sacrifices of those who came before us and to express our gratitude for the people who enrich our lives today. We give thanks for the love of family and friends, the support of our neighbors, and the courage of our veterans who protect our freedoms and way of life. Here in Oklahoma, Thanksgiving also reminds us of the strength of our communities. From rural towns to bustling cities, we come together to support one another, celebrate our shared values, and build a brighter future for generations to come. Whether it’s through acts of service, sharing meals, or simply spending quality time with loved ones, Oklahomans embody the spirit of gratitude and generosity that defines this holiday. Let us also remember the importance of faith during this season. The Pilgrims turned to their faith during their most challenging times, finding strength and hope in their belief in God’s providence. As we gather with our families, let us take a moment to bow our heads and thank God for His many blessings—our homes, our health, and the freedoms we enjoy. Thanksgiving is a time to look back with gratitude and forward with hope. It is a time to celebrate the ties that bind us together as a nation and as a community. While we may face challenges, the spirit of Thanksgiving reminds us of the resilience and unity that have carried us through difficult times. From all of us at the OKGOP, we wish you and your family a safe and Happy Thanksgiving. May your holiday be filled with joy, love, and gratitude. Together, let us give thanks for the privilege of living in the greatest nation in the history of mankind. God bless you, and God bless America. For the People, Nathan Dahm OKGOP Chairman

name: History Facts
subject: Was there pumpkin pie at the first Thanksgiving?
body:
Pumpkin pie has been an American tradition for as long as the United States has had traditions, and we largely have the country’s Indigenous populations to thank for that. [](https://track.historyfacts.com/?xtl=1krv9cfc68c7nog194xrc0d43q4ir7iqcmkv32iln7cv2qb4tdidk5qhj1tc9ps0q5k2h0p5cigkaf84kp6c8z3pimvww4gnq2cfk59behbip8sw2jhp0baxuahmh0dymldkurx2shbyejib9ayqy6n10&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=snntkz&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)

# [Pumpkin pie has Indigenous roots.](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=snntkz&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
### [U.S. History](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=snntkz&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
[](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=snntkz&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
| P umpkin pie has been an American tradition for as long as the United States has _had_ traditions, and we largely have the country’s Indigenous populations to thank for that. Pumpkin itself is [native](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=snntkz&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) to North America and was first cultivated around 5500 BCE. The winter squash was almost certainly introduced to European settlers by the [Wampanoag](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=snntkz&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) people of Massachusetts, who helped the newly arrived colonists at Plymouth survive their first winter. Though it’s unknown who made the first pumpkin pies, we do know the earliest versions [had no crust](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=snntkz&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com), but were cooked (and served) inside hollowed-out pumpkins — an obvious-in-hindsight innovation that some adventurous bakers still prepare on occasion.
---
Pumpkin in all its many forms proved popular with the English, who eventually began cooking pies in the form we know today. And though pumpkin pie was not [served at the first Thanksgiving](https://track.historyfacts.com/?xtl=53p517cis0izbtd4ane2mx8r0hshxrkn9abwhmjmb94jsn7i051ie4a4duoick61yjn6vragtmul5zd7kkzjgyd6qwm74ybyx1uus2mzxcwsigswgijxs9m0s4tyd0903hgo4p4tmzdql0sum0doclpst61sod3d0qoltlubfacsq0vefmrn7z734atwqpbpgn5umfap6oibeg6u2gs8uhz5ok2bwp5uahtrhdm8j35vys70g3wdrwdhjqregtphxfsyph1dbi1rnqup4qozlprc5ns0zeugvope&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=snntkz&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com), it has been a staple of the holiday for centuries; the town of Colchester, Connecticut, went so far as to [delay](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=snntkz&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) the holiday by a week in 1705 because there wasn't enough molasses to prepare the dessert. As with much else in America, including Thanksgiving itself, none of this would be possible without the contributions of Indigenous people. The autumn treat remains popular to this day, with [36%](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=snntkz&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) of Americans citing it as their favorite Thanksgiving pie — more than twice the number that opt for pecan pie (17%) or apple pie (14%) instead.
[](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=snntkz&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) | [](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=snntkz&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)

## No one’s sure how big pumpkins can get.
Pumpkins [keep getting bigger](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=snntkz&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com), with new records set every few years. The current record holder, a pumpkin known as Michael Jordan, weighed 2,749 pounds and was grown in 2023; it dethroned a 2,702-pounder that was grown in Italy two years earlier, which beat a previous 2,624-pound Belgian pumpkin. This could be seen as an example of the Bannister Effect, which suggests that, once a seemingly unbreakable record is broken by one person, it becomes easier for other people to break it as well. But the trend is also evidence that no one actually knows how big pumpkins can get. At the first edition of the Half Moon Bay Safeway World Championship Pumpkin Weigh-Off, which was held in 1974, the winner was 132 pounds. That number seems positively puny now, and it’s possible that the current record holder will likewise be eclipsed before too long — growers are already working on surpassing 3,000 pounds.

name: The Babylon Bee
subject: Trump, Elon, & The Bee
body:
What Americans love more than bacon| | | [](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=b9740a6d67794fb5a9bb7073b6cdaaba&_e=aRAHMGMPM4OxMo38xmEtLJqcZ4bME8GEjcVl1wJCS3Jq8Q1g4M-Q2-AvLRIRRrqoRGt3D2ThCMYbpPzOwfNoVZ7fcmKHlFovJZ8s295In9TM9oLdb354vyA3tQ1Z56qVhtjhcpgziAqtEsc-rCYIV8UnbuJa0yMt9vATnSdBs3sxHiNvv_qVDh9CgB_5HSd2yiuZtxNTj0mRekpUAbF1C2mbCFs41kk6r7y-JRo2NH7R7_qmoZFiJ3iYQhnF3NMVg29_V7PbuR861dpD8szVHw%3D%3D)
---
|
---
Dear Reader, Voters have made their voices heard.Turns out the American people care deeply about two things: the cost of bacon and a censorship regime that silences its critics. For years, the left has used the liberal "news" media to spread their woke agenda, but it was largely behind closed doors. **That all changed when Twitter silenced The Babylon Bee for telling a joke.** The absurdity of a tech giant deplatforming a satirical site woke a sleeping giant.**_Enter Elon Musk._** Elon purchased Twitter and joined The Babylon Bee in starting a revolution that catapulted free speech to the election's frontlines.
---

body:
I will continue to fight this injustice.

[https://secure.winred.com/rudy-giuliani-freedom-fund-79a3adfa/a3323b0d2e57dabb/?utm_campaign=20241127&utm_medium=email&utm_source=RW_B&utm_content=WAMA&utm_term=vbout]

Dear Friend,

The Democrat Deep State wants $146 MILLION from me – all because I
dared to ask questions about the legitimacy of America’s elections.

To comply with such a frivolous demand, I’d have to give up
_EVERYTHING_ I own – including my dignity.

That’s why I have OFFICIALLY APPEALED this outrageous ruling that
would leave me on the streets.

As I stand on the brink of losing everything, I remind myself that the
Bible says that earthly possessions will always fade, but greater
“heavenly treasures” await us.

Truth, justice, and the fight for freedom are worth _infinitely_
more... They are the “heavenly treasures... that cannot be stolen
and will never rust, decay or lose their value.”

AND WHEN I SEE THAT TWO OF THE RIGGED CASES BROUGHT AGAINST PRESIDENT
TRUMP HAVE NOW BEEN DISMISSED AND “DELAYED INDEFINITELY,” I HAVE
HOPE THAT I CAN WIN MINE TOO!

_But my ability to win this appeal and stay in this fight until
justice is served depends entirely on the support given to me by kind
souls like you, Friend._

Let me also say that I know we’re in the midst of the holidays, so
family and friends come first. Please don’t feel pressured to give
if you can’t!

BUT IF YOU CAN FUND MY BATTLE TO APPEAL THIS UNJUST RULING, PLEASE

name: Elizabeth Nader
subject: Happy Thanksgiving
body:
Peter, I hope this email finds you putting the finishing touches on a dinner for loved ones or relaxing by the fire after a d
|
---
| Peter, I hope this email finds you putting the finishing touches on a dinner for loved ones or relaxing by the fire after a day of family and friends.
---
|
---
| We have so much to be grateful for as citizens of the greatest country on earth. Today, my heart is full. I give thanks for my family, for the freedom to build the life I want and worship as I choose, and for the many who sacrificed and continue to sacrifice to safeguard that freedom. I’m also thankful for you and the fearless Common Sense Majority rising up to take back our state. From all of us at Common Sense Club, Happy Thanksgiving to you and yours!

name: Kelly Ayotte
subject: Happy Thanksgiving!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kelly for New Hampshire, Chris Connelly Treasurer, PO Box 4723 Manchester, NH 03108
body:
I hope your day is full of family, friends, laughter, and delicious food. | | | | |
---
| | | | | Friend,
Happy Thanksgiving! I hope your day is full of family, friends, laughter, and delicious food.
Here are a few things I’m thankful for this year — my wingman, Joe, and our amazing kids, Kate and Jake; our new puppy, Thunder; morning runs; and, of course, the opportunity to serve our wonderful state as your next Governor.
I am so thankful for your support over the course of my campaign and as we prepare to take office in January. I couldn’t have done this without you!
Our family is also grateful to live in the greatest country on earth. I want to say a special thank you to all of our military service members, law enforcement, and first responders away from their families on Thanksgiving to protect our communities, our state, and our nation. Please join me in remembering them and thanking them for their service and sacrifice.
Joe, the kids, and I wish you a very Happy Thanksgiving! Thank you for your continued support as we work to keep New Hampshire safe, prosperous and free.
All the best,
Kelly
---

name: Gabe Amo
subject: A time for gratitude
body:
Hi Friend, As you gather with friends and loved ones in this season of thanks, I wanted to share my gratitude for your support and offer a reflection on our journey together.| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/97788667/514597888/-1283252511?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDU0NTUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYjNlOWZhZjktOGZhZC1lZjExLTg4Y2YtNjA0NWJkZmU4ZDI5IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=3jhViHuIs8P7cnyON2EronKm7GJ80BoKm50de3KPjn4=&emci=6eaae725-25ad-ef11-88cf-6045bdfe8d29&emdi=b3e9faf9-8fad-ef11-88cf-6045bdfe8d29&ceid=12466492)
---
| | Hi Friend,
---
As you gather with friends and loved ones in this season of thanks, I wanted to share my gratitude for your support and offer a reflection on our journey together.
---
I remain forever thankful that Rhode Islanders have placed their trust in me to represent them in Congress. It is my honor to serve the First Congressional District every day, from the halls of the House of Representatives to every community between Woonsocket and Newport.
---
I know this has been a tough several weeks for many as we think about our nation’s future. And while the path ahead will not be easy — it never is — I hope you know we are in this together. You have my commitment that I will continue to fight tooth and nail for Rhode Island values and priorities at every turn.
---
Happy Thanksgiving!
---
Forever grateful,Gabe
---
| | |
---

name: API PA
subject: Join Us in the Fight – Support API PA!
body:
https://www.apipennsylvania.org [https://www.apipennsylvania.org]
Hi Peter,
As we reflect on the challenges and opportunities ahead, we want to take a moment to express our heartfelt gratitude. Despite the looming uncertainties ahead, we are incredibly thankful for our vibrant community. Your readiness to stand with us and support one another is what fuels our commitment to fight for justice and equality.
Whether you are a new supporter or have been with us since the beginning, your ongoing support has been invaluable in our battles - past, present, and future. Together, we've made strides in critical political programs at both local and national levels. From our efforts in mental health reform through our Justice for Christian Hall initiatives to our fight to preserve one of Philadelphia's last remaining cultural gems, our beloved Chinatown, from billionaire developers, your contributions have empowered us to rise to the occasion.
No Arena protestors march through the streets in the rain [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/AV/AV001/1/82704/images/20240907-9.7.24%20March.jpg] A group of people hold signs that say ''Justice for Christian Hall'' and ''Mental Health Care is Necessary Care'' [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/AV/AV001/1/82704/images/20241127-Katrina%20Speaking%20at%20Justice%20for%20Christian%20Hall%20Hearing.jpg]
This year has posed its challenges, but thanks to your support, API PA has stepped up to fight for our community, and we will continue to do so. These essential efforts will require resources though.
As we approach Giving Tuesday - a global day of generosity - we invite you to support us with a donation. Your contributions will help ensure that we have the necessary funds to continue our critical work and make a lasting impact.
DONATE [https://www.apipennsylvania.org/donate?am=20&amtOpts=&recurringAm=20&recurringAmtOpts=&is_optimized_ask=true&contactdata=n/+grP/C35f8pLLba1NuII9SH8GA8X/FkhpB+/cGf2KhPssb4ebI5sq76M31zBVGzBW19FcbVC7JEselyPKWAS/DQxqy3kNwfqQLRI0Nc8PNGvckB9zyrnCkF+Jg3tfKucPuEB4WNwljChhc5rJ296O5hEBNID2Y14QicsFo2rc=]
Save the date for Giving Tuesday on December 3rd. Throughout the day, we'll introduce our amazing team and share about the incredible work we've accomplished together this year. Engage with us with your likes, comments, and donations.
Thank you once again for your unwavering support. Together, we can face any challenge ahead.
With gratitude,
Suhee You
Senior Development Manager
Asian Pacific Islander Political Alliance
https://www.facebook.com/apipennsylvania [https://www.facebook.com/apipennsylvania] https://twitter.com/apipennsylvania [https://twitter.com/apipennsylvania] https://instagram.com/apipennsylvania [https://instagram.com/apipennsylvania] https://www.tiktok.com/@apipennsylvania [https://www.tiktok.com/@apipennsylvania]

API PA
PO Box 22611
Philadelphia, PA 19110
United States

name: Nella Domenici
subject: Wishing you a Happy Thanksgiving!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Nella for Senate
body:
No tradition reveals our character as a nation and as individuals as clearly as our celebration of Thanksgiving. | | | | | [](https://nellaforsenate.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6ac79b36261163c3706eb25b8&id=32c8d0b519&e=780129cd82)
---
| | | | | **Wishing you a Happy Thanksgiving!**
---
| As the weather cools, the daylight shortens, farolitos start to deck rooftops and the smell of pinon is in the air, all our senses signal that the season of giving has arrived. I want to express my gratitude for all of you – old friends, new friends, and those who knew and loved my Dad. I want to express my heartfelt kinship with all New Mexicans as we pause to celebrate Thanksgiving 2024.
No tradition reveals our character as a nation and as individuals as clearly as our celebration of Thanksgiving. It is our nature and tradition to extend a helping hand to those in need and to pause from our busy lives to give thanks for our many blessings.
The Thanksgiving tradition is older than our nation itself. Eloquent Thanksgiving Presidential Proclamations are enduring national traditions. Each President has used the occasion as a time for Americans to acknowledge our gratitude as a Nation for our Nation. After the Civil War which threatened the continued existence of our nation, that divided brother against brother, President Lincoln reminded our fragile Country that we needed to reflect on the “return of the inestimable blessings of peace, union, and harmony throughout the land.”
From that time forward, we as citizens of the most imperfectly perfect Republic have rejoiced in our freedom to gather with whom we please safe from war, tyranny and oppression.
This month, much like many Novembers before, we participated in an election and will witness the peaceful transfer of power. While some of you may be elated with the outcome, and others concerned, what ties us together is the ability to still call ourselves United. For that I am grateful.
Closer to home we all have our family Thanksgiving traditions. As I set my table for Thanksgiving, it suggests a metaphor for governing. Everyone is welcome and encouraged to join me at the table. Bring your hearty appetite for hope, aspirations and improvements. Bring your cornucopia of ideas for solutions to our problems, and join me in saying Grace for this wonderful place we all call home — New Mexico.
Have a Happy Thanksgiving, and may God bless you!
_**-Nella Domenici**_

clean_body: No tradition reveals our character as a nation and as individuals as clearly as our celebration of Thanksgiving. | | | | | | | | | | Wishing you a Happy Thanksgiving! | As the weather cools, the daylight shortens, farolitos start to deck rooftops and the smell of pinon is in the air, all our senses signal that the season of giving has arrived. I want to express my gratitude for all of you – old friends, new friends, and those who knew and loved my Dad. I want to express my heartfelt kinship with all New Mexicans as we pause to celebrate Thanksgiving 2024. No tradition reveals our character as a nation and as individuals as clearly as our celebration of Thanksgiving. It is our nature and tradition to extend a helping hand to those in need and to pause from our busy lives to give thanks for our many blessings. The Thanksgiving tradition is older than our nation itself. Eloquent Thanksgiving Presidential Proclamations are enduring national traditions. Each President has used the occasion as a time for Americans to acknowledge our gratitude as a Nation for our Nation. After the Civil War which threatened the continued existence of our nation, that divided brother against brother, President Lincoln reminded our fragile Country that we needed to reflect on the “return of the inestimable blessings of peace, union, and harmony throughout the land.” From that time forward, we as citizens of the most imperfectly perfect Republic have rejoiced in our freedom to gather with whom we please safe from war, tyranny and oppression. This month, much like many Novembers before, we participated in an election and will witness the peaceful transfer of power. While some of you may be elated with the outcome, and others concerned, what ties us together is the ability to still call ourselves United. For that I am grateful. Closer to home we all have our family Thanksgiving traditions. As I set my table for Thanksgiving, it suggests a metaphor for governing. Everyone is welcome and encouraged to join me at the table. Bring your hearty appetite for hope, aspirations and improvements. Bring your cornucopia of ideas for solutions to our problems, and join me in saying Grace for this wonderful place we all call home — New Mexico. | | Dear Fellow American, World War II is the greatest armed conflict in human history, encompassing global fighting in unprecedented ways. We must preserve the lessons learned from this world war.
---
| | That’s why Hillsdale College has produced an online course entitled [“](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=2c9c9a5222b2449a9c0debb32cc3a1e4&_e=s4Kv8pGlfsrckcwpWsEaxkX7ZYkrm9MFmVtZGEZ8d6-GgENVIbhxWXDl6FlWC4YnVoBD-ppHZ4ayfCjwfww1E-bIGKWz_kAc7jYpmSVuZ3NE47MA9xriFCu1HvkYQwgTbLTvqTbsPpDyfn35NRp45etUQBnb_ytV1g7h1gGApy-zv8LGL1qpMclFyOS3lcleE4EkqTfeyI4pDHU4a8QwkIgx5tdlPzrcIbxSEh5NeJsCqu9TZg0zDEAlaCZT9TMPIwZWKN-TQn2F-YI7A0EQWF_DUIzvI7veiR3G5FbYER23FDtMPXTj5OvE8iH5rZN6R59VA3jtvz758smPES9wt9zDYJ4gOlZsb1DZwNTT1NVW_gNK0LoMvTAycz_oMSdYPvp2An_WODVjXaqllH6e2tpTW45u8NHTG75M3Hydtrw%3D)**[The Second World Wars.”](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=2c9c9a5222b2449a9c0debb32cc3a1e4&_e=s4Kv8pGlfsrckcwpWsEaxkX7ZYkrm9MFmVtZGEZ8d6-GgENVIbhxWXDl6FlWC4YnVoBD-ppHZ4ayfCjwfww1E-bIGKWz_kAc7jYpmSVuZ3NE47MA9xriFCu1HvkYQwgTbLTvqTbsPpDyfn35NRp45etUQBnb_ytV1g7h1gGApy-zv8LGL1qpMclFyOS3lcleE4EkqTfeyI4pDHU4a8QwkIgx5tdlPzrcIbxSEh5NeJsCqu9TZg0zDEAlaCZT9TMPIwZWKN-TQn2F-YI7A0EQWE4O5QDDlkbLRcNBq_R-UmD8UG1WHXE7Br0cz5lYslQDEN4loFLgGVHIMah1fLpFbkgVPiNgr_PUYWShm0vbPncElVdYDQW3XuCgHwwVujXOtGAt4kn8R4HzfMqqt6pjvbOj0agobSUPIJk7n4pXff8%3D) **And right now, you can receive your very own DVD box set of this course when you make a tax-deductible gift of $100 or more in support of our educational outreach efforts on behalf of liberty. This limited-edition DVD set provides an analysis from military historian **Victor Davis Hanson** of Allied and Axis investments and strategies in the air, by water, and on land that led to the Allies’ victory. This is one of the most popular Hillsdale courses of all time. And there are only a limited number of DVDs available. If you’d like to secure a DVD set before they’re all gone, you can have one shipped directly to your home for any gift of $100 or more to Hillsdale College.

name: Kelly Morrison
subject: Happy Thanksgiving!
body:
https://kellyforcongress.org [https://kellyforcongress.org]
Hi Peter!
Happy Thanksgiving!
Now more than ever, I am so grateful to all of you. This team knocked on thousands of doors, made thousands of phone calls, and wrote thousands of postcards to voters across MN-03. You helped us raise enough money to run a robust campaign in the field, in the mail, on-line, and on the airwaves. Your work and generosity were critical to winning this seat.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP59/1/109390/images/people/Screenshot%202024-11-27%20at%208.05.49%E2%80%AFPM.png]
I am tremendously honored to be the next representative of this incredible district in Congress. We have challenges ahead to be sure, but there is much to be grateful for. I am ready to get to work, and so thankful to have you on this team.
Wishing you and your family a joyful and relaxing Thanksgiving!
With gratitude,
Kelly Morrison
https://www.facebook.com/KellyMorrisonForCongress [https://www.facebook.com/KellyMorrisonForCongress] https://twitter.com/Morrison4MN [https://twitter.com/Morrison4MN] https://instagram.com/kellymorrisonforcongress [https://instagram.com/kellymorrisonforcongress] kellyforcongress.org [kellyforcongress.org]
Email is the best way to keep our supporters up to date.
If you only want to receive our most important emails, click here to receive fewer emails. [https://secure.ngpvan.com/hHyPtKcXyUeK_xcY7BEsAQ2]

## DEA Considers Loosening Marijuana Restrictions
THE CENTER SQUARE—The Drug Enforcement Administration plans to hold a formal hearing next week on its plan to reschedule marijuana at the federal level. The proposal is to move marijuana from a Schedule I drug, along with heroin and LSD, to Schedule III, where it would join ketamine and anabolic steroids. In May, the Department [...] [Read more](http://links.conservativeum.com/u/click?_t=26c2e4aa9bbe40418f61e26972138f21&_m=bc8a5045b4f442669e475d05c1b6d15f&_e=ayJ8P-qYQgyEjpS4SPbqMjAdVTmEH_tGbv2zUc3uz1rE-dqK3qeZC_tI6p_jABClzTy8i8wjNYG-M5Gpbb3jKVnX1bOxF2BiVDDplr0a_PmE6-_uP70-YPOCTpbbKAAXu79fkbEH9quxgCKAqHuGws-Ht9T9DTUC9CKHS9g9H1_KqVk3qCxLYRmUwpcI-OvcUcxKdqxgcHZML4LPMsLWFFaQjToOwE71e930NhJo1OIePmMQ8BKUog_zpWnH9ZCAuLOIUuLPGaf-cE2bX7EUYg%3D%3D)
---
| |
## A Political Cornucopia to Give Thanks for at Thanksgiving
As families gather around their dining room tables this Thanksgiving, it’s regrettable that some seats won’t be taken because Trump Derangement Syndrome won’t permit its victims to break bread and count their blessings with relatives and friends who don’t share their irrational hatred of once and future President Donald Trump. The hilarious account Libs of [...] [Read more](http://links.conservativeum.com/u/click?_t=26c2e4aa9bbe40418f61e26972138f21&_m=bc8a5045b4f442669e475d05c1b6d15f&_e=ayJ8P-qYQgyEjpS4SPbqMjAdVTmEH_tGbv2zUc3uz1pzrXL98hIo8CkPgDIpVcsgtcdmdaHXzWM4HgUNKuDz7tRviCQvjrhgUl5SjmQrw-DZ1M-FAkslafJoxEO5Reu6EeyJSDpuNOmpoEtwkXK2XhX37C4DsgPSAP2gCdKnyZ4-gngekSCy9TVRO4ltOVtnrLjP7zumGmbdin490t_hGjIA-TZftejitE_MdpSzMNd4UW06lPOoNZGqQ-NiFe971hcuv6JzFUuO6NljyjfGnIcnv4k7uLAs3Ak_AU859us%3D)

## Now Mark Zuckerberg Makes the Pilgrimage to Mar-a-Lago – RedState
Mark Zuckerberg famously co-founded the social media site Facebook and its parent company Meta Platforms and is currently the chairman, chief executive officer, and controlling shareholder of the tech giant. That makes him a mega-billionaire, all of which makes him very powerful, whether we like it or not. In [...] [Read more](http://links.conservativeum.com/u/click?_t=26c2e4aa9bbe40418f61e26972138f21&_m=bc8a5045b4f442669e475d05c1b6d15f&_e=ayJ8P-qYQgyEjpS4SPbqMjAdVTmEH_tGbv2zUc3uz1oebIp-fLO6JcsOqWy6A4MYVBMsnAloJkiyS6tLZ5_O6qcLaAHjW11LCZ1ipBEiush9R8bnthitUrXBr3ffoqzj07xSWeqeV6F46VBl8go4mTW19PCmZ_G3HkuLs3FnXfIQqt9LeDUwKxJDmWkTuHB7QSR4V1ACbFaLjlqxOOdiw3E4V4U9A5yZu3lnK7wZ-U5D7uFAZRpY4ujyeUYWTTkz7CZinV4mP7ZFG9Uzs3txVvtmCFhXt7trqHa3Ug3LsQg%3D)

## Exploring How Trump Can Achieve Mass Deportation of 10 Million
Under President Joe Biden’s failed leadership, the United States has been in the throes of a four-year-long illegal immigrant crisis, during which an estimated more than 10 million people have entered the country. With immigration among the top issues in the 2024 campaign cycle, the American people are sending President-elect Donald Trump, who campaigned on [...] [Read more](http://links.conservativeum.com/u/click?_t=26c2e4aa9bbe40418f61e26972138f21&_m=bc8a5045b4f442669e475d05c1b6d15f&_e=ayJ8P-qYQgyEjpS4SPbqMjAdVTmEH_tGbv2zUc3uz1qQ2D2pOCMIMilCT99wQgqWxKB31Rds5_1wIH2XuGBTyec6NYxdgLNFxKRg2S5K-9i5YrXBN0N9jSjHG4wVwzdoDzjA0f82BjaRXROv0ib2pYrFyFq1cQbUOmImhsWkfKJBNYRy5m__aAhSCH2I6w_lMII8NehQsObpMusQz4zW83J4OpdrYYG3h23I9AoSiQFe8m-ukHSa6naKUYQBU4w7y65RInL6n-iXSqtlRyK4IyXTNPUCv3G7qT1AsWlUG2w%3D)

## Priest stuns America by not answering famous Bible clues on ‘Jeopardy!’ * WorldNetDaily * by Joe Kovacs
Kevin Laskowski on ‘Jeopardy!’ An Episcopal priest who is now a three-day champion on “Jeopardy!” is raising eyebrows after being unable to answer two famous clues from the Holy Bible on Wednesday night’s broadcast. body:
President Trump's victory ignited a firestorm - now NPR's new CEO is doubling down on liberal propaganda, and it's time we cut them off at the source...

| Liquid error: Internal error Liquid error: Internal error Liquid error: Internal error Fellow Conservative, With President Trump's historic victory, it's more crucial than ever that we stand strong against the liberal media's relentless attacks on our America First movement. name: Citizens Alliance New Hampshire
subject: 🦃 Thankful for Liberty, Grateful for You
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CITIZENS ALLIANCE OF NEW HAMPSHIRE]()
body:
Thank you for standing with us, for believing in a freer New Hampshire, and for helping us build a legacy of liberty... | | | | |
---
| | | | [](https://citizensalliancenh.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=9ec7dd6f72fe3fc1550cd2695&id=5f2ce79a9b&e=8c8e704ee6)
---
| Peter,

**As we gather with loved ones this Thanksgiving, we at Citizens Alliance of New Hampshire have so much to be thankful for — starting with YOU!**

Your support made it possible to elect _**71 liberty-loving lawmakers**_ who are committed to upholding the values that make New Hampshire a beacon of freedom.

Because of you, we were able to knock on over 12,000 doors, send more than 200,000 text messages, and run targeted digital campaigns to rally support for our champions.

Each of the 71 candidates we supported has signed our Pledge, committing to sponsor and uphold legislation that defends the principles of the New Hampshire and U.S. Constitutions.

**Thanks to your dedication, we’re entering the next year with a team of champions who will fight for the values that keep New Hampshire a sanctuary of freedom.**
---
| **This Thanksgiving, we are grateful for you...**

Whether you volunteered, donated, or simply stood with us in spirit, your commitment to preserving liberty has been the heart of our mission.

We are also thankful for the principled lawmakers who will stand up for our shared values in the legislature next year.

These lawmakers will bring bold ideas to Concord, fighting for the freedoms we hold dear and building a foundation for a state where liberty thrives.

Next year, we’ll continue building on this momentum, rallying more support and electing even more champions to ensure New Hampshire remains the Live Free or Die state for generations to come.

Thank you for standing with us, for believing in a freer New Hampshire, and for helping us build a legacy of liberty.

>From our family to yours, we wish you a Happy Thanksgiving.
---
[](https://citizensalliancenh.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=9ec7dd6f72fe3fc1550cd2695&id=f2be7a6c53&e=8c8e704ee6)
| Daniel Gray
Executive Director
Citizens Alliance of New Hampshire

name: Steve Kerrigan
subject: What I'm thankful for this Thanksgiving
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Massachusetts Democratic State Committee
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP31/1/93809/images/Logo.png]
Dear Peter,
From all of us at MassDems, l want to wish you and your loved ones a very Happy Thanksgiving. Along with all of you, I cherish this opportunity to come together with family and friends and enjoy the company of those whom I hold most dear.
As Democrats, we greet this Thanksgiving in the wake of an election cycle that did not turn out as any of us would have hoped. Still, I find there are many things, and people, to be grateful for this year.
I’m thankful for our congressional delegation, who are gearing up to spend this next legislative session working to ensure America remains a nation that prioritizes liberty and justice for all.
I’m thankful for our constitutional officers, including Governor Healey and Lt. Governor Driscoll, who have made it clear that Massachusetts will work to protect valued communities in the Commonwealth in the face of potential draconian polices that could cause great harm.
I’m thankful for all elected Democrats across Massachusetts, who take so much pride in representing their constituents with dedication and empathy.
Most of all, I’m thankful for all of you, who spent 2024 knocking doors, making calls, sending texts and putting your heart and soul into the causes you believe in. Massachusetts Democrats have been on the front lines for the fight for the soul of our nation, and I am confident that we will continue to be throughout the next four years.
I hope your Thanksgiving is filled with love, laughter and good food. Let’s find joy in the ones we love most, and get ready to continue the fight.
Onward,
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP31/1/93809/images/Steve%20K%27s%20Signature.png]
Steve Kerrigan
Chair, Massachusetts Democratic Party
https://www.facebook.com/massdems [https://www.facebook.com/massdems] https://twitter.com/massdems [https://twitter.com/massdems] https://instagram.com/massdems [https://instagram.com/massdems] https://www.tiktok.com/@massdems [https://www.tiktok.com/@massdems]
Paid for by the Massachusetts Democratic State Committee
Massachusetts Democratic Party
11 Beacon St
Suite 410
Boston, MA 02108
United States

Friend, Happy Thanksgiving! If you’re like me, you’re probably excited to eat — especially if you did a Turkey Trot this morning. We’re cooking together as a family today, and I can’t wait to sit down and enjoy the food. But before we dig in, I wanted to reach out personally to say: **thank you.** Together, we’re building a movement to make Maryland a state where every single person and family can thrive. I’ve been deeply humbled and honored by the support you’ve shown me, and I am honored to have spent the last two years working to better the lives of all the families across Maryland who are gathering together today. From my and Dawn’s family to yours, happy day of gratitude. name: Abigail
subject: Happy Thanksgiving!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger for Governor
body:
Happy Thanksgiving! I have so much to be thankful for this year, and your support has been on my mind this morning as we start preparing for our family’s Thanksgiving meal later today.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/97799164/514630999/-1898250644?refcode=BLU_EM_ASFG_2024-11-28_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDM4NTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiODk1ZjdjODMtOTlhZC1lZjExLTg4Y2YtNjA0NWJkZmU4ZDI5IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=f7ver8LhaSkt0TDPiaD90JAEs9BGgZbSgY-_5NdbVq8=)
---
| Peter,

Happy Thanksgiving! I have so much to be thankful for this year, and your support has been on my mind this morning as we start preparing for our family’s Thanksgiving meal later today.

Since we launched our campaign last November, I’ve been blown away by the graciousness of people like you – people who supported me in my tough races in VA-07 and who are now supporting my latest campaign for Governor of Virginia.

Your unwavering support has allowed me to take this latest step in service to our Commonwealth.

With you in my corner, Peter, we’ve accomplished so much for the Commonwealth. Looking ahead, I’ll continue doing what I’ve always done – working tirelessly for the needs of our communities.

From my family to yours, Happy Thanksgiving.

With gratitude,
Abigail
---

name: Bob Casey
subject: Happy Thanksgiving
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY Pennsylvania Senate Victory Fund 2024
body:
# [](https://go.bobcasey.com/go/181?t=1&akid=4889%2E552475%2Ee3oguA)
Happy Thanksgiving, Peter. This holiday is an opportunity to express our appreciation and gratitude for those in our lives.
I am grateful for time spent with family and friends over this holiday, for the opportunity to continue serving my constituents in Pennsylvania in the next few weeks, and, in particular, to our service members, many of whom will be spending this holiday season protecting us.
Best wishes to you and your loved ones for a happy Thanksgiving.
With gratitude,
Bob Casey

name: Debbie Mucarsel-Powell
subject: I'm grateful for you, friend.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Debbie for Florida
body:
Taking a moment to count the blessings that filled my cup throughout 2024 ...
| | |
---
Good morning, friend, and happy Thanksgiving!
It's been awhile since you last heard from me, so before I get back to prepping for the big feast this afternoon — and the big Dolphins game tonight — I had to take a moment to count the blessings that filled my cup throughout 2024.
I'm grateful for the support of my husband, my kids, my mom, and even our dog, Kali, while I was away from home on the campaign trail.
I'm grateful for my staff who worked day in and day out, rain or shine, to run such a well-oiled campaign machine in every corner of the Sunshine State.
I'm grateful for the voters who helped me win my primary and showed up for me and abortion rights in Florida — _even if our state-level GOP couldn't take majority rule for an answer._
I'm especially grateful that, despite Democrats losing Senate control, Rick Scott will NOT be the majority leader come January.
**But above all, friend, this Thanksgiving, I'm grateful for you.**
Every petition you signed.
Every call you made. Every door you knocked.
Every rally you attended.
Every single cent you contributed.
Every little thing you did helped put my ads on the air, my name in people's mouths, and my race in Florida on the map. **And that, friend, is something that no one — not Donald Trump, not Ron DeSantis, and certainly not Rick Scott — can _ever_ take away from us.**
I hope your holiday is full of light, loved ones, laughter, and lots of good food. Thank you again for all you did — and all you do — in the fight to make Florida a state where our families can thrive. And thank you for never giving up!
_Con gratitud,_

Debbie Mucarsel-Powell
|
* * *

name: Sharice Davids
subject: So much to be thankful for
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY SHARICE FOR CONGRESS
body:
I wanted to take a moment to share my gratitude for you and for this entire team

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/97800752/514635232/-240735101?refcode=email-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1Yjk3NGM5ZS05YWFkLWVmMTEtODhjZi02MDQ1YmRmZThkMjkiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=uKWafO2_x54VFujGoWTBLzBYPMzkGPl8oitTWQjSrTs=) This Thanksgiving, I wanted to take a moment to share my gratitude for you, and for this entire team. Serving our community is the honor of my life. It’s something I wake up grateful for every day. My work wouldn’t be possible if folks like you hadn’t fought for it, and I can’t thank you enough. Despite the challenges ahead, I’m also grateful for the real progress we’ve delivered together. We’ve kickstarted over 300 construction projects in our state, expanded broadband access to rural communities, and created thousands of good-paying jobs across Kansas. Your support has been the key to securing this progress. **But I want you to know that our work is far from finished.** This Thanksgiving, I am deeply thankful for this team — but we must continue to roll up our sleeves and put in the work. No matter what comes next, I promise to keep doing what I’ve always done: work tirelessly for the people of Kansas. With you in my corner, I know we can continue to find ways to make things better for Kansas families and folks across this nation. So before you sit down with family and loved ones today, I want to say **thank you.** I am so, so thankful for your support — today and every day. Happy Thanksgiving, Sharice Davids

---

This Thanksgiving, I am deeply thankful for this team — but we must continue to roll up our sleeves and put in the work. No matter what comes next, I promise to keep doing what I’ve always done: work tirelessly for the people of Kansas. With you in my corner, I know we can continue to find ways to make things better for Kansas families and folks across this nation. So before you sit down with family and loved ones today, I want to say thank you. I am so, so thankful for your support — today and every day. Happy Thanksgiving, Sharice Davids | This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a key way for us to stay in touch and make sure you get the latest updates from our campaign. But if you would like to . | | = | PAID FOR BY SHARICE FOR CONGRESS Sharice for Congress 13851 W. 63rd St. #303 Shawnee, KS 66216 United States

# [Thangstaken and indigenous resilience](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=152267296&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUyMjY3Mjk2LCJpYXQiOjE3MzI4MDY3NTksImV4cCI6MTczNTM5ODc1OSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.8kgjzqVngreI7rbFdRCh2IIuYjWthoxUHyWJIViFI0c)
### A recent trip to New Mexico led me to discover a timeless community pursuing an inspiring ecorecovery project
| | [Shahid Buttar](https://substack.com/@shahidbuttar)

---|---|---

---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=152267296&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUyMjY3Mjk2LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTczMjgwNjc1OSwiZXhwIjoxNzM1Mzk4NzU5LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTAxODU5NyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.Qm2TdvKEzqRPHzxNxKeP-bCiITHEr9R2xra16IPWNtI&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=152267296&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUyMjY3Mjk2LCJpYXQiOjE3MzI4MDY3NTksImV4cCI6MTczNTM5ODc1OSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.8kgjzqVngreI7rbFdRCh2IIuYjWthoxUHyWJIViFI0c)
