---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/shahidbuttar/p/thangstaken-and-indigenous-resilience?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

Today's holiday prompts a complicated mix of feelings. Gratitude is always a good thing to feel and express, while a holiday rooted in racist exploitation and ongoing theft is an ironic excuse to embrace it.
My previous posts commemorating today's holiday have explored the [voices of indigenous youth](https://substack.com/redirect/19e624e9-77b1-45c6-8ccd-bcf112de9485?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), the back yard [garden](https://substack.com/redirect/d302c4b3-f1a3-417b-aed4-1714518e840d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) I cultivated before leaving San Francisco, and my reasons for gratitude in both [2022](https://substack.com/redirect/5b2ea109-9f28-46e2-9ac6-f81b1f5ccb0c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [2023](https://substack.com/redirect/3ec3850d-6297-41dc-87e7-37ec24b3ba60?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
A few days after the "Pueblo Spinning, Weaving, & Creativity" event, we visited the [Taos Pueblo](https://substack.com/redirect/ce83e1f7-fc0f-44aa-8057-f1d8898e96dc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). It is the oldest continuously inhabited community in the United States, having stood for roughly 1,000 years.
I highly recommend a visit, for several reasons.
I've collected those posts below, followed by a few reflections on a recent trip that offered me a new lens on indigenous resilience, environmental stewardship, and intergenerational wisdom.
## [Thanksgiving, Thangs Taken, and Why I'm Grateful Today](https://substack.com/redirect/5b2ea109-9f28-46e2-9ac6-f81b1f5ccb0c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Shahid Buttar](https://substack.com/profile/10904314-shahid-buttar)| | *
---|---|---
| November 24, 2022
[](https://substack.com/redirect/5b2ea109-9f28-46e2-9ac6-f81b1f5ccb0c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[I've taken to calling today's holiday #ThangsTaken, in a reference to its colonial and ultimately genocidal origins. I'll share a video below offering a painfully poignant explanation by indigenous youth of an often overlooked history, before returning to some reasons why I feel gratitude today despite the racist roots of the holiday.](https://substack.com/redirect/5b2ea109-9f28-46e2-9ac6-f81b1f5ccb0c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/5b2ea109-9f28-46e2-9ac6-f81b1f5ccb0c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
## [Why I'm grateful despite the history of #ThangsTaken](https://substack.com/redirect/3ec3850d-6297-41dc-87e7-37ec24b3ba60?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Shahid Buttar](https://substack.com/profile/15484941-shahid-buttar)| | *
---|---|---
| November 23, 2023
[](https://substack.com/redirect/3ec3850d-6297-41dc-87e7-37ec24b3ba60?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[The colonial origins of Thanksgiving are not usually the first thing that Americans consider when celebrating today's holiday. But they're tough to overlook in a year threatening to repeat yet another indigenous genocide, while bearing witness to an accelerating](https://substack.com/redirect/3ec3850d-6297-41dc-87e7-37ec24b3ba60?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/3ec3850d-6297-41dc-87e7-37ec24b3ba60?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
### Indigenous culture & environmental stewardship
Earlier this month, I visited Taos, New Mexico for the first time. A work trip twenty-five years ago gave me an excuse to visit Santa Fe, but this was the first time I had the opportunity to explore more of the state, or to meet and learn from local indigenous elders and youth.
_Thanks for reading Chronicles of a Dying Empire! You can help inform your friends by sharing this post._
[ Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=152267296&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUyMjY3Mjk2LCJpYXQiOjE3MzI4MDY3NTksImV4cCI6MTczNTM5ODc1OSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.8kgjzqVngreI7rbFdRCh2IIuYjWthoxUHyWJIViFI0c)
The night after I arrived, a friend brought me to the Taos Center for the Arts for a [fascinating event](https://substack.com/redirect/f8970b98-742a-48ce-9c6d-06c2a1e1d94c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) exploring "Pueblo Spinning, Weaving, & Creativity." It included demonstrations of several traditional weaving techniques developed by tribes from across North America, as well as the process of spinning wool into yarn.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/8f510882-2736-440e-be43-eb83653a4f13?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
A churro sheep featured in "A Gift From Talking God (The Navajo Sacred Sheep)"
As it happens, we had visited a store earlier in the day that sells yarn, in which the proprietress showed us how a loom works. Between her demonstration and the discovery later that evening of an even more ancient technique, I emerged with a new appreciation for fabric, clothing, and the animals on which humanity long relied to provide them for us.
After the weaving demonstrations, we were grateful to share a native meal of mutton soup seasoned with blue corn jelly, before a documentary screening about an important ecorestoration project undertaken by the [Navajo/Dine people](https://substack.com/redirect/eec3eb9c-1c2a-4906-938a-56b10eb3a3cb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). The event concluded with a riveting panel discussion among an intergenerational group of shepherds.
_Chronicles of a Dying Empire is a publication supported by 55 readers (for whose support I feel deeply grateful), and read by over 21,000. To receive new posts,**sign up for a free subscription!** To enable my work, **please consider becoming a paid subscriber.**_
[ Upgrade to paid](https://substack..X7R6rj8qXOT8bV2kAhnKXaPG_GUrUuVPuhLe03Iksnw?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=152267296)
The film that we saw, "A Gift From Talking God (The Navajo Sacred Sheep)," is [available on YouTube](https://substack.com/redirect/078eb73c-6e90-4dd0-8d42-74afe4d800bc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and runs half an hour. It explores how generations of Navajo shepherds have cultivated the Churro sheep, a subspecies that has played a crucial role in tribal history, as well as the local ecosystem, but was [driven to near-extinction--twice](https://substack.com/redirect/438db7ca-0816-4e6a-a2e9-88091d711fe0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--by the federal government.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/078eb73c-6e90-4dd0-8d42-74afe4d800bc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The film explores the interdependence and mutual reliance of the Navajo/Dine people and the sheep on which they relied for milk, meat, clothing, and sustenance through brutal mountain winters. It also depicts the culture among Navajo/Dine shepherds and their ancient work as environmental stewards.
One theme of the film that struck me related to how indigenous communities treat the animals on whom they depend with such striking reverence. The [relationship](https://substack.com/redirect/0d5ff932-65ce-4844-8ea8-e8b3e388a267?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) between the nomadic tribes of the great plains and the bison herds on which they long relied was one I had learned a few things about before. But until last week, I never knew how sheep performed a similar role for sister communities in the west who lived less nomadic, and more pastoral, lives.
Part of the ensuing discussion that I found engrossing related to gender. Whereas western civilization has traditionally separated all people into two genders--and only more recently embraced alternative identities within, between, and transcending them--the Navajo /Dine people have long identified no fewer than [five (and according to the panelists, even six) genders](https://substack.com/redirect/8977f15e-5e0e-43fb-b791-f76661135e80?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Some of their people, particularly those that Americans might describe as LGBTQ individuals, have been especially respected, and recruited for work as shepherds precisely because they are less inclined towards procreation.
Perhaps the most poignant part of the night was a reflection by a panelist on genocide and its impacts. I'd always thought of indigenous cultural traditions and practices destroyed by the imperial westward expansion as "lost." But the elder on the panel described how he sees them as merely "asleep," waiting for future generations to rediscover and revive them. Recalling the notion sends chills down my spine even now, as I write.
### A millennium-old indigenous community in Taos
A few days after the "Pueblo Spinning, Weaving, & Creativity" event, my friends & I visited the [Taos Pueblo](https://substack.com/redirect/ce83e1f7-fc0f-44aa-8057-f1d8898e96dc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). It is the oldest continuously inhabited community in the United States, having stood for roughly 1,000 years.
I highly recommend a visit, for several reasons.
First, I was surprised to see indigenous American structures that have stood for centuries. My previous exposure to indigenous communities had been largely limited to those with nomadic histories and less permanent architecture, but the two most iconic structures in the Taos pueblos are at least centuries older than the United States.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/3e09eba2-e9e8-402c-b465-168bc74c0f85?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Taos Pueblo / Source: Wikimedia
Second, it was an amazing experience to meet indigenous artisans and learn about their work in the settings where they practiced it. We met people dedicated to making drums, hats, and food, and learned about [occasional events open to the public](https://substack.com/redirect/60b7a45f-4c48-46d5-91fe-f7874097707f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for which I hope to have a chance to visit again.
Finally, the culinary traditions of the Navajo/Dine tickled my palate, and were mostly new to me despite having persisted for far longer than any kind of food I've ever thought of as American. From corn jelly to chili jam, and fry bread to mutton soup and sumac pudding, I was thrilled to discover in an ancient culture what seemed to me like such new cuisine.
That visit to New Mexico, like a [very different trip to a different desert](https://substack.com/redirect/e909ca85-aa15-49e9-acdf-476c7542fbbe?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) earlier this year, and the [dramatic changes in my life](https://substack.com/redirect/0bfd295e-10f3-41e1-8fe3-d3a6bec33434?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) since leaving San Francisco, are among the memories for which I feel gratitude today.
_Paid subscribers can gain access to a brief discussion exploring the recent election and its legitimacy, particularly addressing data collected by Elon Musk 's PAC that may have been used to generate fraudulent ballots._
### Was the 2024 election stolen?...

From corn jelly to chili jam, and fry bread to mutton soup and sumac pudding, I was thrilled to discover in an ancient culture what seemed to me like such new cuisine. That visit to New Mexico, like a earlier this year, and the since leaving San Francisco, are among the memories for which I feel gratitude today. # [Thangstaken and indigenous resilience](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=152267296&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUyMjY3Mjk2LCJpYXQiOjE3MzMwNjY4NzYsImV4cCI6MTczNTY1ODg3NiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.RwLRew8QMMrW42_XSVYOXbVcsSNpn2BL_Fk84ghdrXE)
### A recent trip to New Mexico led me to discover a timeless community pursuing an inspiring ecorecovery project
| | [Shahid Buttar](https://substack.com/@shahidbuttar)

| Dec 1

| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/shahidbuttar/p/thangstaken-and-indigenous-resilience?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)

Today's holiday prompts a complicated mix of feelings. Gratitude is always a good thing to feel and express, while a holiday rooted in racist exploitation and ongoing theft is an ironic excuse to embrace it.
My previous posts commemorating today's holiday have explored the [voices of indigenous youth](https://substack.com/redirect/19e624e9-77b1-45c6-8ccd-bcf112de9485?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), the back yard [garden](https://substack.com/redirect/d302c4b3-f1a3-417b-aed4-1714518e840d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) I cultivated before leaving San Francisco, and my reasons for gratitude in both [2022](https://substack.com/redirect/5b2ea109-9f28-46e2-9ac6-f81b1f5ccb0c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [2023](https://substack.com/redirect/3ec3850d-6297-41dc-87e7-37ec24b3ba60?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
A few days after the "Pueblo Spinning, Weaving, & Creativity" event, we visited the [Taos Pueblo](https://substack.com/redirect/ce83e1f7-fc0f-44aa-8057-f1d8898e96dc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). It is the oldest continuously inhabited community in the United States, having stood for roughly 1,000 years.
I highly recommend a visit, for several reasons.
I've collected those posts below, followed by a few reflections on a recent trip that offered me a new lens on indigenous resilience, environmental stewardship, and intergenerational wisdom.
## [Thanksgiving, Thangs Taken, and Why I'm Grateful Today](https://substack.com/redirect/5b2ea109-9f28-46e2-9ac6-f81b1f5ccb0c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Shahid Buttar](https://substack.com/profile/10904314-shahid-buttar)| | *
---|---|---
| November 24, 2022
[](https://substack.com/redirect/5b2ea109-9f28-46e2-9ac6-f81b1f5ccb0c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[I've taken to calling today's holiday #ThangsTaken, in a reference to its colonial and ultimately genocidal origins. I'll share a video below offering a painfully poignant explanation by indigenous youth of an often overlooked history, before returning to some reasons why I feel gratitude today despite the racist roots of the holiday.](https://substack.com/redirect/5b2ea109-9f28-46e2-9ac6-f81b1f5ccb0c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

## [Why I'm grateful despite the history of #ThangsTaken](https://substack.com/redirect/3ec3850d-6297-41dc-87e7-37ec24b3ba60?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Shahid Buttar](https://substack.com/profile/15484941-shahid-buttar)| | *
---|---|---
| November 23, 2023
[](https://substack.com/redirect/3ec3850d-6297-41dc-87e7-37ec24b3ba60?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[The colonial origins of Thanksgiving are not usually the first thing that Americans consider when celebrating today's holiday. But they're tough to overlook in a year threatening to repeat yet another indigenous genocide, while bearing witness to an accelerating](https://substack.com/redirect/3ec3850d-6297-41dc-87e7-37ec24b3ba60?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/3ec3850d-6297-41dc-87e7-37ec24b3ba60?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
### Indigenous culture & environmental stewardship
Earlier this month, I visited Taos, New Mexico for the first time. A work trip twenty-five years ago gave me an excuse to visit Santa Fe, but this was the first time I had the opportunity to explore more of the state, or to meet and learn from local indigenous elders and youth.
_Thanks for reading Chronicles of a Dying Empire! You can help inform your friends by sharing this post._
[ Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=152267296&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUyMjY3Mjk2LCJpYXQiOjE3MzMwNjY4NzYsImV4cCI6MTczNTY1ODg3NiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.RwLRew8QMMrW42_XSVYOXbVcsSNpn2BL_Fk84ghdrXE)
The night after I arrived, a friend brought me to the Taos Center for the Arts for a [fascinating event](https://substack.com/redirect/f8970b98-742a-48ce-9c6d-06c2a1e1d94c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) exploring "Pueblo Spinning, Weaving, & Creativity." It included demonstrations of several traditional weaving techniques developed by tribes from across North America, as well as the process of spinning wool into yarn.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/8f510882-2736-440e-be43-eb83653a4f13?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
A churro sheep featured in "A Gift From Talking God (The Navajo Sacred Sheep)"
As it happens, we had visited a store earlier in the day that sells yarn, in which the proprietress showed us how a loom works. Between her demonstration and the discovery later that evening of an even more ancient technique, I emerged with a new appreciation for fabric, clothing, and the animals on which humanity long relied to provide them for us.
After the weaving demonstrations, we were grateful to share a native meal of mutton soup seasoned with blue corn jelly, before a documentary screening about an important ecorestoration project undertaken by the [Navajo/Dine people](https://substack.com/redirect/eec3eb9c-1c2a-4906-938a-56b10eb3a3cb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). The event concluded with a riveting panel discussion among an intergenerational group of shepherds.
_Chronicles of a Dying Empire is a publication supported by 55 readers (for whose support I feel deeply grateful), and read by over 21,000. To receive new posts,**sign up for a free subscription!** To enable my work, **please consider becoming a paid subscriber.**_
[ Upgrade to paid](https://substack..Xwi_STByy9SjmjeImL0az5XcSVnwNqapDWM69WRYAOo?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=152267296)
The film that we saw, "A Gift From Talking God (The Navajo Sacred Sheep)," is [available on YouTube](https://substack.com/redirect/078eb73c-6e90-4dd0-8d42-74afe4d800bc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and runs half an hour. It explores how generations of Navajo shepherds have cultivated the Churro sheep, a subspecies that has played a crucial role in tribal history, as well as the local ecosystem, but was [driven to near-extinction--twice](https://substack.com/redirect/438db7ca-0816-4e6a-a2e9-88091d711fe0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--by the federal government.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/078eb73c-6e90-4dd0-8d42-74afe4d800bc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The film explores the interdependence and mutual reliance of the Navajo/Dine people and the sheep on which they relied for milk, meat, clothing, and sustenance through brutal mountain winters. It also depicts the culture among Navajo/Dine shepherds and their ancient work as environmental stewards.
One theme of the film that struck me related to how indigenous communities treat the animals on whom they depend with such striking reverence. The [relationship](https://substack.com/redirect/0d5ff932-65ce-4844-8ea8-e8b3e388a267?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) between the nomadic tribes of the great plains and the bison herds on which they long relied was one I had learned a few things about before. But until last week, I never knew how sheep performed a similar role for sister communities in the west who lived less nomadic, and more pastoral, lives.
Part of the ensuing discussion that I found engrossing related to gender. Whereas western civilization has traditionally separated all people into two genders--and only more recently embraced alternative identities within, between, and transcending them--the Navajo /Dine people have long identified no fewer than [five (and according to the panelists, even six) genders](https://substack.com/redirect/8977f15e-5e0e-43fb-b791-f76661135e80?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Some of their people, particularly those that Americans might describe as LGBTQ individuals, have been especially respected, and recruited for work as shepherds precisely because they are less inclined towards procreation.
Perhaps the most poignant part of the night was a reflection by a panelist on genocide and its impacts. I'd always thought of indigenous cultural traditions and practices destroyed by the imperial westward expansion as "lost." But the elder on the panel described how he sees them as merely "asleep," waiting for future generations to rediscover and revive them. Recalling the notion sends chills down my spine even now, as I write.
### A millennium-old indigenous community in Taos
A few days after the "Pueblo Spinning, Weaving, & Creativity" event, my friends & I visited the [Taos Pueblo](https://substack.com/redirect/ce83e1f7-fc0f-44aa-8057-f1d8898e96dc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). It is the oldest continuously inhabited community in the United States, having stood for roughly 1,000 years.
I highly recommend a visit, for several reasons.
First, I was surprised to see indigenous American structures that have stood for centuries. My previous exposure to indigenous communities had been largely limited to those with nomadic histories and less permanent architecture, but the two most iconic structures in the Taos pueblos are at least centuries older than the United States.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/3e09eba2-e9e8-402c-b465-168bc74c0f85?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Taos Pueblo / Source: Wikimedia
Second, it was an amazing experience to meet indigenous artisans and learn about their work in the settings where they practiced it. We met people dedicated to making drums, hats, and food, and learned about [occasional events open to the public](https://substack.com/redirect/60b7a45f-4c48-46d5-91fe-f7874097707f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for which I hope to have a chance to visit again.
Finally, the culinary traditions of the Navajo/Dine tickled my palate, and were mostly new to me despite having persisted for far longer than any kind of food I've ever thought of as American. From corn jelly to chili jam, and fry bread to mutton soup and sumac pudding, I was thrilled to discover in an ancient culture what seemed to me like such new cuisine.
That visit to New Mexico, like a [very different trip to a different desert](https://substack.com/redirect/e909ca85-aa15-49e9-acdf-476c7542fbbe?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) earlier this year, and the [dramatic changes in my life](https://substack.com/redirect/0bfd295e-10f3-41e1-8fe3-d3a6bec33434?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) since leaving San Francisco, are among the memories for which I feel gratitude today.
_Paid subscribers can gain access to a brief discussion exploring the recent election and its legitimacy, particularly addressing data collected by Elon Musk 's PAC that may have been used to generate fraudulent ballots._
### Was the 2024 election stolen?
Among the numerous articles I've read over the past few weeks addressing the recent election, one has stood out as especially alarming. Not only are its findings indescribably important, but they have somehow been matched by the seeming refusal of anyone in a position of influence to acknowledge them.
Planet: Critical
[Cyber-Security Experts Warn Election Was Hacked](https://substack.com/redirect/877e71e6-78cd-40e9-bd09-69e385adda00?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Cyber-security experts across America are raising the alarm of wide-scale election fraud securing Trump's victory -- and the data is compelling...
[Read more](https://substack.com/redirect/877e71e6-78cd-40e9-bd09-69e385adda00?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
12 days ago * 2387 likes * 517 comments * Rachel Donald
On the one hand, Democrats have a political interest in defending election integrity, particularly against right wing attacks claiming everything from [voter fraud](https://substack.com/redirect/a9c831ff-261e-4fe1-beeb-411b25de4599?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) (which ironically appears to be [most prominent](https://substack.com/redirect/4b7b9ec0-8d1b-4d22-b3ce-ede82ed601c1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) among conservatives) to [hacking](https://substack.com/redirect/f55be21b-0489-43ba-996a-e0f7bc821797?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) as reasons to be skeptical of election results favoring Democrats.
Of course, when elections appear to favor Republicans, like the one earlier this month, those concerns fall largely silent.
But when the threats appear real, they behoove us all to address them. At least in 2000, Democrats supporting Sen. Al Gore's presidential campaign [challenged](https://substack.com/redirect/4769e231-a648-4f08-b111-09686524a0df?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the disputed election results. Although that effort fell on the shoals of the right wing Supreme Court--which [unconstitutionally determined its own future composition 24 years ago by choosing the president who went on to appoint the Justices' successors](https://substack.com/redirect/5d5a1dce-f9e0-43eb-be81-dff17eb8fdb7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--the _Bush v. Gore_ litigation at least represented an attempted defense of election integrity, which is more than Kamala and her supporters have mustered in 2024 despite raising $1 billion in a matter of weeks.
You're currently a free subscriber to [Chronicles of a Dying Empire](https://substack.com/redirect/ab0fb289-0b26-41b2-9afc-d3dd193d015b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). For the full experience, [upgrade your subscription.](https://substack..Xwi_STByy9SjmjeImL0az5XcSVnwNqapDWM69WRYAOo?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=postcta)

Thanks for reading Chronicles of a Dying Empire! You can help inform your friends by sharing this post. The night after I arrived, a friend brought me to the Taos Center for the Arts for a exploring "Pueblo Spinning, Weaving, & Creativity." It included demonstrations of several traditional weaving techniques developed by tribes from across North America, as well as the process of spinning wool into yarn. | | ---|---|--- A churro sheep featured in "A Gift From Talking God (The Navajo Sacred Sheep)" As it happens, we had visited a store earlier in the day that sells yarn, in which the proprietress showed us how a loom works. Between her demonstration and the discovery later that evening of an even more ancient technique, I emerged with a new appreciation for fabric, clothing, and the animals on which humanity long relied to provide them for us. After the weaving demonstrations, we were grateful to share a native meal of mutton soup seasoned with blue corn jelly, before a documentary screening about an important ecorestoration project undertaken by the . The event concluded with a riveting panel discussion among an intergenerational group of shepherds. Chronicles of a Dying Empire is a publication supported by 55 readers (for whose support I feel deeply grateful), and read by over 21,000. To receive new posts,sign up for a free subscription! To enable my work, please consider becoming a paid subscriber. The film that we saw, "A Gift From Talking God (The Navajo Sacred Sheep)," is and runs half an hour. It explores how generations of Navajo shepherds have cultivated the Churro sheep, a subspecies that has played a crucial role in tribal history, as well as the local ecosystem, but was the federal government. The film explores the interdependence and mutual reliance of the Navajo/Dine people and the sheep on which they relied for milk, meat, clothing, and sustenance through brutal mountain winters. It also depicts the culture among Navajo/Dine shepherds and their ancient work as environmental stewards. One theme of the film that struck me related to how indigenous communities treat the animals on whom they depend with such striking reverence. The between the nomadic tribes of the great plains and the bison herds on which they long relied was one I had learned a few things about before. But until last week, I never knew how sheep performed a similar role for sister communities in the west who lived less nomadic, and more pastoral, lives. Part of the ensuing discussion that I found engrossing related to gender. Whereas western civilization has traditionally separated all people into two genders--and only more recently embraced alternative identities within, between, and transcending them--the Navajo /Dine people have long identified no fewer than . Some of their people, particularly those that Americans might describe as LGBTQ individuals, have been especially respected, and recruited for work as shepherds precisely because they are less inclined towards procreation. Perhaps the most poignant part of the night was a reflection by a panelist on genocide and its impacts. I'd always thought of indigenous cultural traditions and practices destroyed by the imperial westward expansion as "lost." But the elder on the panel described how he sees them as merely "asleep," waiting for future generations to rediscover and revive them. Recalling the notion sends chills down my spine even now, as I write. A millennium-old indigenous community in Taos A few days after the "Pueblo Spinning, Weaving, & Creativity" event, my friends & I visited the . It is the oldest continuously inhabited community in the United States, having stood for roughly 1,000 years. I highly recommend a visit, for several reasons. First, I was surprised to see indigenous American structures that have stood for centuries. My previous exposure to indigenous communities had been largely limited to those with nomadic histories and less permanent architecture, but the two most iconic structures in the Taos pueblos are at least centuries older than the United States. | | ---|---|--- Taos Pueblo / Source: Wikimedia Second, it was an amazing experience to meet indigenous artisans and learn about their work in the settings where they practiced it. We met people dedicated to making drums, hats, and food, and learned about for which I hope to have a chance to visit again. Finally, the culinary traditions of the Navajo/Dine tickled my palate, and were mostly new to me despite having persisted for far longer than any kind of food I've ever thought of as American. From corn jelly to chili jam, and fry bread to mutton soup and sumac pudding, I was thrilled to discover in an ancient culture what seemed to me like such new cuisine. That visit to New Mexico, like a earlier this year, and the since leaving San Francisco, are among the memories for which I feel gratitude today. Paid subscribers can gain access to a brief discussion exploring the recent election and its legitimacy, particularly addressing data collected by Elon Musk 's PAC that may have been used to generate fraudulent ballots. Was the 2024 election stolen? Among the numerous articles I've read over the past few weeks addressing the recent election, one has stood out as especially alarming. Not only are its findings indescribably important, but they have somehow been matched by the seeming refusal of anyone in a position of influence to acknowledge them. Planet: Critical Cyber-security experts across America are raising the alarm of wide-scale election fraud securing Trump's victory -- and the data is compelling... 12 days ago 2387 likes 517 comments * Rachel Donald On the one hand, Democrats have a political interest in defending election integrity, particularly against right wing attacks claiming everything from (which ironically appears to be among conservatives) to as reasons to be skeptical of election results favoring Democrats. Of course, when elections appear to favor Republicans, like the one earlier this month, those concerns fall largely silent. But when the threats appear real, they behoove us all to address them. At least in 2000, Democrats supporting Sen. Al Gore's presidential campaign the disputed election results. Although that effort fell on the shoals of the right wing Supreme Court--which Bush v. Gore litigation at least represented an attempted defense of election integrity, which is more than Kamala and her supporters have mustered in 2024 despite raising $1 billion in a matter of weeks. name: Beto O'Rourke
subject: Thoughts on Thanksgiving
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY POWERED BY PEOPLE
body:
[1]Powered By People

I live in a city that is defined by its connection to the rest of the
world, in a state that determines the future of this country, and in a
nation that, at its core, still aspires to equality, liberty and freedom
for all.

Though none of these places are perfect and the world presents challenges
that our forefathers could not have dreamed of, we are lucky to be alive
at this place and time... even with, and maybe especially because, our
country stands on the edge of the end of what Lincoln called The Last Best
Hope of Earth.

Precious few generations have had the chance to fight to save and fully
realize the ideals that make America so special. Think of the patriots who
won our freedom from the British empire; the brave souls who gave their
lives to end slavery and restore the Union; those who landed under heavy
fire on Normandy beaches 80 years ago to fight fascism abroad in order to
protect democracy here at home; and the freedom marchers and riders who
risked their lives to ensure our country could live up to its promise of
equality under the law. Like those generations, we are faced with a stark
choice: submit to tyranny (the Empire; the Confederacy; the Nazis; Jim
Crow) or stand and fight.

You are reading this because you understand the grave task before us, the
extreme danger of a new, emerging tyranny in America. I don’t have to
spell out what we’re up against or the consequences to future generations
if ours is found wanting.

So to you and every person willing to do what they can with what they have
where they are: I am forever grateful. We are a people who choose to
fight, no matter the odds, no matter the setbacks. Those who came before
have shown us the way and remind us what we are made of.

Let’s enjoy the days ahead — time with family and friends, time to relax
and recharge, time to count our blessings — and emerge ready for the
fight.

Happy Thanksgiving to you and your family from ours,

Beto

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to act. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1001&akid=239905%2E444211%2E9DyDIh

PAID FOR BY POWERED BY PEOPLE

name: The Daines Family
subject: Happy Thanksgiving
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Steve Daines for Montana
body:
From my family to yours, Happy Thanksgiving! |
---

Fellow American,

As we gather with our loved ones this Thanksgiving, I want to take a moment to express my heartfelt gratitude to you. Your dedication, support, and belief in the values we share inspire me every day.

This season reminds us of the blessings we enjoy together. May your Thanksgiving be filled with joy, love, and cherished moments with family and friends.

From my family to yours, Happy Thanksgiving!

subject: Happy Thanksgiving
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dan Goldman for New York**
body:
|
#
---
Happy Thanksgiving, Peter. While we as a nation continue to grapple with the results of the election, this Thanksgiving is a much-needed opportunity to take stock of everything we have to be grateful for. I’m thankful for my family, which has been an incredible support system and maintains my perspective. I’m thankful for the voters of New York’s 10th Congressional District for entrusting me with the privilege of representing them in Congress for a second term. And I’m especially grateful for you and for this grassroots team, Peter. Whether you open and read these emails, sign my petitions, or even chip in a few dollars here and there, you are essential to our movement. Your support reflects our deep commitment to our democracy and the rule of law. Together, we will never stop working toward a stronger and more just future for every New Yorker and every American. As I’m sure you know, our work is far from over. The fight ahead to protect and preserve our democracy and the rule of law will not be an easy one. But today, I just want to express my gratitude that you are on our team. | | Fellow Conservative, As someone who's lived and worked in Hollywood my whole life, I want to say: **_You are absolutely right if you think Hollywood is reaching right into your family living room with "entertainment" that grooms children._** Today when children turn on the TV, they see a Drag Queen leading the Pride Parade on _Blue's Clues_...

| | And the result of this constant propaganda? An upside-down world where children lecture you on what you can and cannot say......and they try to "educate" YOU about your "old-fashioned" and "mean" ideas. But you and I know your ideas aren’t old-fashioned or mean at all. They are the same biblical values that made America the greatest country on earth. **That’s why I’m reaching out to you. Today you have a very special chance to stop the Hollywood groomers who are turning our children and grandchildren against us and the Godly values we love. ** **[**Today, I'm inviting you to be a Founding Supporter of the very first children's television show that actively fights back against the groomers in Hollywood AND reinforces the traditional biblical values that made America great.**](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=d37f0558fb4b443395c781a74bd5f0d0&_e=I3Qv_1SnmC1ljYlOgbUTf5FYQaumuW6lJFH7Sn1CDomd_MAU4ZYlYYFIoIY5TLU_jQmqvuCCC8UIUVTamQs7RjkSNM_puOTVYLGPOnhfJDY-Nqg89l8gRFLceEpgz1PozJQtdLPWWXF3gLI7Gay9PGfoQ-b9iYAAXitmW6BovJ3-QsJGEtBYIomZNigqKYIca5L7Lz2l-VG5iiROEbhtoQBNewrU6cauagPa5UTQDcGxjmBzDTrzdL7TiiRERz80MG9jSGV9IxA-15JYxqbTl9WvUJQ53O0QJcn7yHPaBQaCT8e3aKbxyY_h5dZGH0bSfJXhbunWXkKUdaAhkPKVAv8UDQjrv-azzR0HbOvtfyO6MpPoY-NFhO2p09UnT0b_tsVpm0AMAknfO6ezjHMbBQ%3D%3D)** And it's only possible with the help of motivated Christians who want to see a Christian resurgence in America like you. Introducing: _Adventures with Iggy and Mr. Kirk_. This ground-breaking live-action television show captivates children... ...but more importantly, it builds their character at the same time. Imagine a children's show that leaves your child or grandchild with a greater sense of right and wrong and an understanding of truth. Courage, honesty, discernment, forgiveness, empathy, all taught through a biblical and traditional lens. Imagine a children's show that brings families together instead of driving them apart. Now imagine YOU are the one who makes that show possible. **[**Because I can't beat the Hollywood groomers without your help as a Founding Supporter.**](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=d37f0558fb4b443395c781a74bd5f0d0&_e=I3Qv_1SnmC1ljYlOgbUTf5FYQaumuW6lJFH7Sn1CDomd_MAU4ZYlYYFIoIY5TLU_jQmqvuCCC8UIUVTamQs7RjkSNM_puOTVYLGPOnhfJDY-Nqg89l8gRFLceEpgz1PozJQtdLPWWXF3gLI7Gay9PGfoQ-b9iYAAXitmW6BovJ3-QsJGEtBYIomZNigqKYIca5L7Lz2l-VG5iiROEbhtoQBNewrU6cauagPa5UTQDcGxjmBzDTrzdL7TiiRERz80qWqxYiMGZjTYJO-1j1nYyUgd8OEPoWpNS7t1gW8DZhlsAlEVuNwxUTxTMdK-UnuZs-AVzK-KlAlSDjLlss1ytqulOMCq5lq92rJjr0JhK8faSUn7IifbjKUrvBK6gnD70FDOa4tzhNyGT-8tugQedw%3D%3D)** Season One of _Adventures with Iggy and Mr. Kirk_ has ten lesson-filled episodes planned. And each episode costs around $70,000 to produce. I know that's a lot of money but it's actually a fraction of what it would cost if we made our show with a major Hollywood studio. And obviously we won’t work through Hollywood. Hollywood has done quite enough already to destroy American culture and hijack our children. We parents, grandparents, and concerned citizens must take over to rebuild a thriving, healthy, GOOD and GODLY culture. **[That's why I'm asking you to contribute $35, $50, $250, $500, or more to help us reach our fundraising goal of $70,000 per episode.](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=d37f0558fb4b443395c781a74bd5f0d0&_e=I3Qv_1SnmC1ljYlOgbUTf5FYQaumuW6lJFH7Sn1CDomd_MAU4ZYlYYFIoIY5TLU_jQmqvuCCC8UIUVTamQs7RjkSNM_puOTVYLGPOnhfJDY-Nqg89l8gRFLceEpgz1PozJQtdLPWWXF3gLI7Gay9PGfoQ-b9iYAAXitmW6BovJ3-QsJGEtBYIomZNigqKYIca5L7Lz2l-VG5iiROEbhtoQBNewrU6cauagPa5UTQDcGxjmBzDTrzdL7TiiRERz80Qccv7J1_CHw7_dQLWYpLdLIlGUdjVGqgQIi05bkHCzAtl15f6N5bqX4hPmkaFdUPYZMNqU_8zdlwCcHrx-UMSNbnloD_jjnI33KS1c0A7RhOr_bf1p0XGXqG9m66STxLZOOJDy6b5B3nTt_jiwR71g%3D%3D) ** Whether you can send $35, $50,000, or anything in between, or anything higher or lower -- please know that gifts of EVERY size are needed and greatly appreciated. As your special "Thank You" for joining me as a **Founding Supporter** at the $35 and above level, we’ll send you**** all ten episodes of** _Adventures with Iggy and Mr. Kirk_ **for your family to enjoy**** FREE on DVD.** ** Hollywood groomers have destroyed America's Godly culture. But today we rebuild from the ashes. And it starts by ensuring the most innocent among us are filled with faith and family -- not filth. Thank you for joining us as a **Founding Supporter. **

clean_body: Hollywood groomers have destroyed America's Godly culture. But today we rebuild from the ashes. | | | You are absolutely right if you think Hollywood is reaching right into your family living room with "entertainment" that grooms children. | | | | Fellow Conservative, As someone who's lived and worked in Hollywood my whole life, I want to say: You are absolutely right if you think Hollywood is reaching right into your family living room with "entertainment" that grooms children. Today when children turn on the TV, they see a Drag Queen leading the Pride Parade on Blue's Clues... | | And the result of this constant propaganda? An upside-down world where children lecture you on what you can and cannot say......and they try to "educate" YOU about your "old-fashioned" and "mean" ideas. But you and I know your ideas aren’t old-fashioned or mean at all. They are the same biblical values that made America the greatest country on earth. That’s why I’m reaching out to you. Today you have a very special chance to stop the Hollywood groomers who are turning our children and grandchildren against us and the Godly values we love. And it's only possible with the help of motivated Christians who want to see a Christian resurgence in America like you. Introducing: Adventures with Iggy and Mr. Kirk. This ground-breaking live-action television show captivates children... ...but more importantly, it builds their character at the same time. Imagine a children's show that leaves your child or grandchild with a greater sense of right and wrong and an understanding of truth. Courage, honesty, discernment, forgiveness, empathy, all taught through a biblical and traditional lens. Imagine a children's show that brings families together instead of driving them apart. Now imagine YOU are the one who makes that show possible. Season One of Adventures with Iggy and Mr. Kirk has ten lesson-filled episodes planned. And each episode costs around $70,000 to produce. I know that's a lot of money but it's actually a fraction of what it would cost if we made our show with a major Hollywood studio. And obviously we won’t work through Hollywood. Hollywood has done quite enough already to destroy American culture and hijack our children. We parents, grandparents, and concerned citizens must take over to rebuild a thriving, healthy, GOOD and GODLY culture. Whether you can send $35, $50,000, or anything in between, or anything higher or lower -- please know that gifts of EVERY size are needed and greatly appreciated. As your special "Thank You" for joining me as a Founding Supporter at the $35 and above level, we’ll send you** all ten episodes of Adventures with Iggy and Mr. Kirk for your family to enjoy FREE on DVD. Hollywood groomers have destroyed America's Godly culture. But today we rebuild from the ashes. And it starts by ensuring the most innocent among us are filled with faith and family -- not filth. Thank you for joining us as a Founding Supporter. | | Kirk CameronActor, Author, and Father | | | Kirk Cameron**Actor, Author, and Father | | | | | Brave Books 13618 Poplar Cir, Suite 302Conroe, TX 77304

| | Dear Friend, Today I need your help to defend myself against the Left’s “lawfare” before they can financially destroy my family.All I ask is that you **[Rush a “FIRST AMENDMENT FUND” Emergency contribution before midnight tonight.](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=750dbc0408b649ce848a72f149b4e503&_e=MSzQlkQaHpYd0cAuCybG1-e_1Bju6PYq9vLLPk23YAU72J4vjsqSa2YN4WoTk1MX_xtSXybFO39QafWkJ9juul2Kkfc7wVOlq__60dd-iPR59FfVzpsnz3vQIu0KatHEyQR64sAqn_tRpACSx2Do5flqBuq1eePvez9Xjmtb_fVVMbeVKjrP_yqdoiwDkV76fKKcnwXXW7al6CDhD_hT0du7UI2iSobgR_edW4e6jFTN_Y7Kqd3WBykge88hPrpsMvtIeVO52o3TL-wb1vLcOWJxygwmNLJXxAhNyaE2ldEXwxw7bM5HFTJif0jGkrDs4io5K9eOuJ45mQm0W3jhiJpDQIdLFvzxY0yh_ILKYsA25NyoDQ_7qnyxRc7B5RaRKa3D-ES_plzirthL3qS8vdk6GkoYEO0jx1ZgBGwyyZ4%3D)** When an Arizona Election Official went on the record promising to _“Make life hell”_ for me, I could have never imagined it would have been this bad...I incurred hundreds of thousands of dollars in expenses to defend myself in this defamation case – and I'm afraid this battle is only getting started. Think about it. The Left wanted to force me to pay a multi-million-dollar legal judgment because I told the truth and called out election fraud.Everything I’ve done has been for election integrity.Watching Arizona botch yet another election, while silencing my legitimate questions about fraud, is a slap in the face to every Arizona voter and conservative American. It’s a national embarrassment. We have to change it.But if standing up and demanding change means you’re going to be personally and financially destroyed, we are never going to fix our elections.That’s what happened to me. Each day that the weight of this lawfare feels too much to bear, I think about President Trump and how he takes every attack head-on and never once thinks about backing down.Just think about what’s happening to him.Radical Never-Trump prosecutors and judges threatened to take everything away from him -- his businesses, his buildings, his homes, and even his freedom. **And he fights on.** So, if the Left thinks that some liberal-packed court threatening me with a hundred-million-dollar fine will get me to back down from this fight, **then they know _nothing_ about me.**And they know nothing about _you_ , too.I’m in this fight for my kids, Ruby and Leo, and my husband, Jeff.This is about protecting their rights, giving them a prosperous America to live in, and defending our family name.To me, that was worth the millions of dollars this could have cost.Because nothing in this world can come between me and my family.Not the Left’s lawfare. Not power-drunk liberal prosecutors.Not litigious, partisan hacks who hate the truth.Thankfully, a generous group has stepped up and is willing to help me cover my legal expenses.So today, I must ask you to contribute to this new**“First Amendment Fund”** to help me defeat the Left’s sham lawfare. [**My friend, could you rush back a generous, tax-deductible gift of $35, $50, $100, $250, or more to help me cover the court fees, lawyers, expert witnesses, counsels, and so much more?**](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=750dbc0408b649ce848a72f149b4e503&_e=MSzQlkQaHpYd0cAuCybG1-e_1Bju6PYq9vLLPk23YAU72J4vjsqSa2YN4WoTk1MX_xtSXybFO39QafWkJ9juul2Kkfc7wVOlq__60dd-iPR59FfVzpsnz3vQIu0KatHEyQR64sAqn_tRpACSx2Do5flqBuq1eePvez9Xjmtb_fVVMbeVKjrP_yqdoiwDkV76fKKcnwXXW7al6CDhD_hT0du7UI2iSobgR_edW4e6jFTN_Y7Kqd3WBykge88hPrpsMvtIeVO52o3TL-wb1vLcOZ31sWYwxLFio6S8ffv8Jfbs588k2nE9YGM3skJm6qlzmuTR7q6HYbcyFfDJ3IQLcOP03z9TxfovpEYvf5kXPZrx8f7WMqI577YIPWqGZc4DNJq7JuuG0GiDM1icthq8oKiB2xgPmJnSeb3NVfLLDO4%3D) The Left wanted this case to destroy my name and credibility. They also wanted to force me and my family into bankruptcy.

| | Folks – Happy Thanksgiving! Thanksgiving is truly one of my favorite holidays of the year. This weekend gives us lots of time to rest before the New Year, spend time with our close family and friends, and reflect on what we're grateful for. For me, I am grateful for my two beautiful kids (three if you count Roxy, our dog!), my friends, the many blessings in my life, and each and every one of you. I can't thank you enough for the support you've shown during our campaign. So many Delawareans stood behind us and our mission of **Bright Hope**. It fills me with joy and inspiration to see that we're uniting folks from all walks of life. Because of you, we get to bring **Bright Hope** to the Senate. This Thanksgiving, I hope you have the opportunity to spend time with your loved ones as I am. Now, I have to run to set the table and get ready for dinner! So from my family to yours, we're wishing you a warm and restful Thanksgiving. Lisa
---
* * *
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/97803145/514641465/631174349?refcode=LOC_LBR_EM_FR_2024_Email-Footer&source=LOC_EM_NOFR_2024.11.28_Thanksgiving_B1_F1_S1_C1_30OP_N-T1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjg3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMTJkZjhiNC05ZGFkLWVmMTEtODhjZi02MDQ1YmRmZThkMjkiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=OD9y8Lzm_kO8cLaoQcxfLPuB1HV4ieTZE6Q2U_NnWag=) I officially became the first woman and person of color to **_ever_** represent Delaware in the Senate. name: The Hon. J.D. Crouch II | CEO & President | USO
subject: On this day of gratitude …
body:
Thank you for supporting our heroes.
[](https://www.uso.org)
[GIVE NOW](https://links.e.uso.)
---
Happy Thanksgiving. I won't take up too much of your time today, but I did want to share a personal note of thanks for all you do throughout the year to help the USO support our service members and their families.
[](https://links.e.uso.org/els/v2/6jKZHVeqV-Fv/UllUVlBjdUFpZDFkci95VXhDZFhUQW0yS3l1WVEycTV4ekpSTW15dTdaZjRSTnRnbVQ4K2o4T20wRkFmajBDSy9BWEpHWE1Kdm5PYld2d3BIZUNWbDNZeEljei8rL2pKNUN6TkNOdGVhS3M9S0/ZUxsdGhSNHMxWGpVbzROYmtZZ29Hc3V3WE10d1MxNlo2KzlCRjNXeStRWWxyRWVKN3RQWVlJNjV5bGNXaTJ1NTl6NGQ1Q0t3M3EyYnU5V09xbmlFTnRraHZyMWlhbHd3S0)
The USO proudly stands beside our heroes in uniform as they make countless sacrifices to serve our country. From long deployments overseas to responding when natural disasters strike here at home, they give up special days like today with their loved ones. Our work **is only possible with this community 's generous support.**
Because of you, thousands of service members who can't be with their families this Thanksgiving can enjoy a warm, festive meal courtesy of the USO. They'll be able to video chat with their families, virtually read a bedtime story to their kids and maybe even watch some football at their local USO Center. **In moments like these, the USO becomes a home away from home.**
Every day, we work closely with each branch of the military to understand the needs of our service members and provide the support that will make a difference.
**With your continued support, we 'll keep expanding and evolving -- because there's always more we can do to be there for our heroes and their families.**
**[As you reflect on your blessings this holiday, I hope you'll consider making a Thanksgiving gift to honor our military. Your contribution of $29 or more will show appreciation for our heroes on this day of gratitude and help the USO continue providing critical support.](https://links.e.uso.)**
[GIVE NOW](https://links.e.uso.org/els/v2/~8eXC0jv0asG/UllUVlBjdUFpZDFkci95VXhDZFhUQW0yS3l1WVEycTV4ekpSTW15dTdaZjRSTnRnbVQ4K2o4T20wRkFmajBDSy9BWEpHWE1Kdm5PYld2d3BIZUNWbDNZeEljei8rL2pKNUN6TkNOdGVhS3M9S0/ZUxsdGhSNHMxWGpVbzROYmtZZ29Hc3V3WE10d1MxNlo2KzlCRjNXeStRWWxyRWVKN3RQWVlJNjV5bGNXaTJ1NTl6NGQ1Q0t3M3EyYnU5V09xbmlFTnRraHZyMWlhbHd3S0)
---
From all of us at the USO, and the thousands of service men and women you are helping, thank you for your generosity, compassion and patriotism. I hope you have a wonderful Thanksgiving.
J.D. Crouch II
CEO & President, USO
United Service Organizations, Inc. (USO) is a nonprofit 501(ς)(3) organization (EIN/Tax ID: 13-1610451) and not part of the Department of Defense (DOD). The appearance of DOD visual information does not imply or constitute DOD endorsement.

God Bless, Josh Hawley | | | | Paid for by Josh Hawley Victory Committee | |

name: Glenn Grothman
subject: Happy Thanksgiving!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Glenn Grothman for Congress
body:
| | | My friend, Happy Thanksgiving! As we gather with family and friends around the Thanksgiving table, I want to express how incredibly grateful I am to once again represent you in Congress. As I reflect on today, I am filled with gratitude for my wonderful family, the precious freedoms we cherish, and the support you've shown me.

So, from my family to yours, I extend my warmest wishes for a joyous and fulfilling Thanksgiving celebration!

Sincerely, **Glenn Grothman**
---

name: Squad Victory Fund
subject: Happy Thanksgiving, Peter! 🦃🍂
body:
[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/18910?refcode=em20241128-1134-foote&t=1001&akid=1134%2E342411%2EzpDpW4 ]Squad Victory Fund

Happy Thanksgiving,
Peter! 🦃🍂

There's so much that our Squad is thankful for this year, and as we
reflect on it all, one thing stands out above all: YOU!

This movement wouldn’t be possible without your support, and that is not
an exaggeration. You have all stood by us, when MAGA Republicans attacked
our values, our visions for the future, our characters, and more. And you
continued to have faith in the world we’re working to build together.

While we know there’s a lot of work to be done, we find constant
reassurance knowing that we have you on our side. Together, we will
continue to fight for our rights, our futures, and our planet.

Thank you for being a part of this movement. We couldn’t do it without
you.

Thankful for you,

The Squad Victory Fund

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20241128-movement.yJhZ-X&refcode=em20241128-1134&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1002&akid=1134%2E342411%2EzpDpW4 ]Contribute ››

In addition to what’s legally required, The Squad Victory Fund does not accept contributions from: (1) corporations, associations, or their PACs; (2) individuals registered as federal lobbyists or under FARA; (3) from the PACs, lobbyists, or SEC-named executives of fossil fuel companies; or (4) from the Fraternal Order of Police.

Contributions or gifts to The Squad Victory Fund are not tax deductible.

Contributions to The Squad Victory Fund will be allocated as follows: 8.33% to each of Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, Rooted in Community Leadership PAC, Cori Bush for Congress, Politivist PAC, Jamaal Bowman for Congress, and For the Children PAC.

Any contributor may designate their contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if any contributor makes a contribution that, when allocated, would exceed the amount that the contributor may lawfully give to any participant.

After contributing to The Squad Victory Fund, you will automatically be signed up to receive email updates from Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Cori Bush for Congress, and Jamaal Bowman for Congress. You may of course opt-out by unsubscribing at any time.

To contribute via check, please address to The Squad Victory Fund c/o MBA Consulting Group, PO Box 15845, Washington D.C., 20003

Email us: info@squadvictoryfund.com

name: Lizzie Pannill Fletcher
subject: Grateful
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ELIZABETH PANNILL FLETCHER FOR CONGRESS
body:
ELIZABETH PANNILL FLETCHER FOR CONGRESS [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/82676/images/lizzie-reelect-logo-outlined.png]
This Thanksgiving, as we gather with friends and family, we have the opportunity to celebrate all we are grateful for. I want to take a moment to let you know that you are one of them.
# [#]
Since 2017, I have had the privilege of working with so many people who are committed to serving our country and making our world a better place. From my colleagues in Congress to my neighbors in TX-07 and across Houston, I am thankful for all those who are working in the ways they can to deliver on the promise of a better tomorrow. I am honored by and thankful to the people of TX-07, who have entrusted me to represent them in this important work.
Of course, I am grateful to my friends and supporters, near and far, who supported me in my re-election campaigns this year—in March and again in November. So many people were a part of this team again this year, contributing their time, money, and energy to getting out the vote—making phone calls, writing postcards, knocking on doors, and showing up everywhere to make a difference. We have some incredible leaders in our community, and I am grateful to work with all of you.
As we look ahead to 2025, I am as committed as ever to continuing the work of defending democracy, ensuring equality, and restoring our rights together. We know that our disappointments in other races this November mean that the work ahead will not be easy, and will have profound impacts on our community and country. But, I will be ready to continue our work together.
This Thanksgiving day, and every day, I am grateful for your friendship, commitment, and patriotism. It is with that in mind and gratitude in my heart that I wish you and your loved ones a peaceful and happy day of Thanksgiving.
Together, we can do anything,
LizzieSignature [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/82676/images/lizzie_signature-1.png]
3262 Westheimer, PMB 636, Houston, TX 77098
PAID FOR BY ELIZABETH PANNILL FLETCHER FOR CONGRESS

name: Josh Gottheimer
subject: Thankful
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JOSH GOTTHEIMER FOR GOVERNOR
body:
# [#] Happy Thanksgiving, team!

This Thanksgiving, we must come together and remember to be thankful for our Jersey communities. Every one of our neighbors deserves family, friends, and plenty of joy (and turkey)! I’m also grateful for those serving our country at home and abroad who can’t be with their families today.

This year, and every year, I am so grateful for you! Your support and generosity have gotten me to where I am. As we head into this next journey and my campaign for Governor, I want to thank you. It is people like you that remind me why Jersey is the best state in the country!

From my family to yours, Happy Thanksgiving!

Josh
Josh is running to be the next Governor of New Jersey in 2025 – the “Lower Taxes, Lower Costs Governor.” Since 2016, his campaign lawn signs have always said the same thing: “Lower Taxes. Jersey Values” – and that’s what he’s done, from expanding the Child Tax Credit to fighting to restore SALT to clawing dollars back to Jersey for first responders. name: Adam Schiff
subject: Thanksgiving greetings
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY SCHIFF FOR SENATE
body:
|
# [](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/61?t=1&akid=8201%2E5257348%2EnXE-Me)
Hi Derek —
I want to take some time to wish you a happy Thanksgiving and express my gratitude to you for standing with me through the most challenging moments of my professional career.
Whether you’ve donated to my campaign, volunteered, given me your feedback, or just simply read emails like this one, your support has been the foundation of everything we’ve achieved together.
Running for the U.S. Senate was an extraordinary journey, and I learned so much from each and every one of you. From your struggles to your successes, your inspiration and support are what fuel my commitment to deliver for every Californian.
As Senator-Elect, I promise to fight every day to uphold our shared values, strengthen our economy, protect our democracy, and ensure that California continues to lead the way for our country.
Thank you, Derek, for believing in me and for granting me the opportunity to fight for you.
Wishing you and your loved ones a joyful Thanksgiving!
— Adam

name: Ruben Gallego
subject: Grateful for you, Peter.
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Gallego for Arizona
body:
#

Peter,
I hope you’re all able to spend some time with your dearest friends and family today. After a long (very long) campaign and a few days in overtime until our race was called, I certainly am.
And before I waste any more of your time, I just wanted to let you know that as I reflect on the things I’m most grateful for today — my wife, my kids, my mom and my sisters, and the life I’ve fought for — you’re on the list, too.
I often told people that we couldn’t depend on a small army of millionaires and billionaires as we campaigned all over Arizona. It was the support of a massive team that got me over the finish line. You are a part of that team, Peter.
And I will never forget that it is because of your support that I am able to fight for Arizona in the United States Senate.
So, with that, enough from me. Get back to whatever you were doing, and give my best to everyone you’re celebrating with today.
Happy Thanksgiving,
Ruben

Paid For By Gallego for Arizona

subject: Happy Thanksgiving

body:
---
| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/97807655/514651997/941995357?refcode=20241121_em_ex_b1_np4c_main_grassroots_SF&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDIvMS84ODQ2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhYTUzMTRlMS1hMWFkLWVmMTEtODhjZi02MDQ1YmRmZThkMjkiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qBXxuuPvciXxOa9EWn2JdNPiweWbaYFQzLyuqNatJwA=&emci=0c614d92-dcac-ef11-88cf-6045bdfe8d29&emdi=aa5314e1-a1ad-ef11-88cf-6045bdfe8d29&ceid=16320219)

Dear Friends,
It’s Nancy, wishing you and your families a healthy and happy Thanksgiving!
To my constituents in San Francisco and friends around the country: as we count our blessings on Thanksgiving, we share gratitude for the community we have built together and for our freedoms that our men and women in uniform sacrifice for every day.
Each and every one of us has a stake in the future of our country and that means that we have a stake in the wellness and safety of one another and are called to recognize the spark of divinity in each and every one of us. I am also grateful for all of our fellow Americans who are welcoming strangers at their table, not just on Thanksgiving but in public policy.
You, our Volunteers in Politics, make all the good that we do in Congress possible with your outside mobilization because you believe in being a channel and instrument of peace in how you live your life and your activism.
In that spirit of looking out for one another and building peace, your neighbors and your country are depending on your compassion, commitment, courage and patriotism.
Thank you for all that you do. God bless you and God Bless America.
Happy Thanksgiving!

Speaker Emerita Nancy Pelosi is the U.S. Representative for California’s 11th District. She's fighting to help working families and protect America from the dangerous agenda of House Republicans. We know you're getting a lot of emails -- but it's only because what we're facing right now as a country is so important. **TO: Special Counsel Jack Smith
FROM: Peter Willis**
Subject: I stand with you.

**Dear Special Counsel Jack Smith,

_I am FURIOUS that Trump is trying to take out his revenge on you!_**

You’ve done an incredible job working to hold him accountable and uphold the law – so it’s no surprise MAGA Republicans are attacking you at every turn.

We know Trump must be TERRIFIED for your final report to be released, so he’s doing everything he can to attack you.

Peter,

Happy Thanksgiving! Take time today to celebrate with your loved ones, eat good food, and reflect on what you’re grateful for.

Although the last few weeks have been full of uncertainties, we are so grateful for the support of all of you. Community members like you have given us so much in the last year, and we’re so thankful.

We hope you and your family enjoy a wonderful Thanksgiving!

– PA Dems

name: Autism Health
subject: Happy Thanksgiving from Autism Health Inc!
body:
|
---
| |
---
|
---
| | Dear Supporters, As we gather with family and friends this Thanksgiving, we want to take a moment to express our heartfelt gratitude to you, our incredible supporters. Your dedication, generosity, and unwavering commitment to the autism community inspire us daily. Whether you've contributed your time, resources, or words of encouragement, you are the foundation of our mission to create a brighter future for individuals and families impacted by autism. This year has been remarkable, filled with progress, milestones, and moments of hope. From successful events like the Autism Health Summit to the countless connections made through advocacy and education, your support has made an undeniable difference. It is because of you that we can provide resources, build a stronger community, and advocate for lasting change. As we reflect on these achievements, we are reminded of the power of unity and compassion in transforming lives. As we look ahead to 2025, we are excited about the opportunities to continue our mission and make an even greater impact. Together, we are planning new programs, growing our outreach, and preparing for another incredible [Autism Health Summit](https://autismhealth.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7c0b21e245e40197b3670bea2&id=2de370b36f&e=3a42edfdc4) in April. With your support, we know the future holds even more possibilities for connection, healing, and empowerment. This Thanksgiving, we give thanks for you, our supporters, who make everything we do possible. May your holiday be filled with joy, love, and gratitude. From all of us at [Autism Health Inc.](https://autismhealth.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7c0b21e245e40197b3670bea2&id=9952f97766&e=3a42edfdc4), we wish you a wonderful Thanksgiving surrounded by those you cherish. With gratitude,
The Autism Health Inc. Team
---

clean_body: | | | | | | Dear Supporters, As we gather with family and friends this Thanksgiving, we want to take a moment to express our heartfelt gratitude to you, our incredible supporters. Your dedication, generosity, and unwavering commitment to the autism community inspire us daily. Whether you've contributed your time, resources, or words of encouragement, you are the foundation of our mission to create a brighter future for individuals and families impacted by autism. This year has been remarkable, filled with progress, milestones, and moments of hope. From successful events like the Autism Health Summit to the countless connections made through advocacy and education, your support has made an undeniable difference. It is because of you that we can provide resources, build a stronger community, and advocate for lasting change. As we reflect on these achievements, we are reminded of the power of unity and compassion in transforming lives. As we look ahead to 2025, we are excited about the opportunities to continue our mission and make an even greater impact. Together, we are planning new programs, growing our outreach, and preparing for another incredible in April. With your support, we know the future holds even more possibilities for connection, healing, and empowerment. This Thanksgiving, we give thanks for you, our supporters, who make everything we do possible. May your holiday be filled with joy, love, and gratitude. From all of us at , we wish you a wonderful Thanksgiving surrounded by those you cherish. With gratitude, The Autism Health Inc. Team | "Click Here for More Information" | | YOUR DONATIONS MAKE A DIFFERENCE! Autism Health Inc. depends on generous donations from our community. Large or small, every donation gets us closer to achieving our goals. We are grateful for your assistance! | "DONATE NOW" | Autism Health is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization dedicated to educating individuals, families, and communities about autism spectrum disorder (ASD). name: Jena Griswold
subject: Happy Thanksgiving, Peter!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jena for Colorado
body:
https://jenaforcolorado.com/ [https://jenaforcolorado.com/]Peter –
Happy Thanksgiving! Whether you are getting outside for a local neighborhood turkey trot, gathering around the TV for the Thanksgiving Day Parade, heading to the backyard for a game of football, or spending the day baking and cooking the dishes we all love, I hope this day is full of warmth and gratitude.
I am thankful for so much this year, especially for my husband and our son. I am also thankful for the opportunity and honor to serve the state of Colorado as Secretary of State. Together we have accomplished so much, and I know that in Colorado we will continue to strengthen our democracy and protect the values we hold dear.
Wishing you a wonderful day with friends and family!
Jena Griswold

name: Harriet Hageman
subject: I’m thankful for you
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY HAGEMAN FOR WYOMING**
body:
Here’s why:
| | | | [](https://link.updates.hageman4wyoming.)
---
In 2022, I sent Liz Cheney packing.

She turned her back on the Republican Party, President Trump, and most importantly, the AMERICAN PEOPLE.

So, on Election Day that year, I took her office.

_**This is Congresswoman Harriet Hageman.**_

For years, I’ve stood by President Trump - and the MAGA movement showed up for him this November with the greatest victory in American History!
We’ve got a REPUBLICAN HOUSE, SENATE, AND WHITE HOUSE, **all thanks to Patriots like YOU!**
America is one step closer to returning to the powerhouse it once was. Already, we’re seeing promises being kept, and a future that’s hopeful for us, our children, and our grandchildren.
This Holiday season, I wanted to show my appreciation for YOU.
Without you by our side, we couldn’t have pulled off the Greatest Comeback in American History. If you would like to donate to show your support,**[I’m including a link here.](https://link.updates.hageman4wyoming.)**
If you're struggling to make ends meet right now, please disregard this and go about your day.

Rest assured, I will keep fighting tooth and nail against the Democrats in D.C. to put an end to the Biden-Harris inflation that’s hurting hard working Americans like you.

Together, we will make America Strong, Safe, and Healthy Again. The Best is Yet to Come!
Thank you & God Bless.
Congresswoman Harriet Hageman

[DONATE](https://link.updates.hageman4wyoming.)

| | Peter, during this Thanksgiving, it might be hard to feel thankful, especially after this month’s election results. But despite it all – **I am thankful and hopeful today**. I’m thankful for my amazing family. I’m thankful for every person who supported my campaign to fight for everyday Floridians. I’m thankful for all the organizers and volunteers who worked overtime to try and make tomorrow a better day. **And I’m thankful to the grassroots supporters, like YOU, who helped me bring home a victory on Election Day and power our organizing every day.** So on a day where it might feel hard to be thankful for the state of our country – I’m going to focus on what we can control and what there is to be thankful for. I hope that wherever you are you’re able to find your own joy on a day that’s about bringing people together. And if you’re able, I would be so thankful if you could contribute to support my work towards progress in Congress, for all of us. If you’d like to give, here’s a link for you to use: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/maf-em-pge?refcode=20241128_em_fr_b1_thanksgiving_en&amount;=27 Happy Thanksgiving, Maxwell Frost

subject: Happy Thanksgiving
body:
What are you most Thankful for!!

# Happy Thanksgiving
### What are you most Thankful for!!
| | | [Dan Carr](https://substack.com/@dancarr2014)
---
| Nov 28
---
| [](https://substack.com/@dancarr2014)

name: Andrea Salinas
subject: Happy Thanksgiving!
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Andrea Salinas for Oregon
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/97809678/514658352/-1080040549?refcode=email-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNjc1OTMyYy1hOGFkLWVmMTEtODhjZi02MDQ1YmRmZThkMjkiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=RRvcc3gyCZitsRE3nng9Uyma9whB2PetvMkTs_5byxI=) I hope you and your loved ones have a joyful and relaxing Thanksgiving! This holiday is a time for reflection, and despite the tough challenges facing us on the road ahead, I’m overwhelmed with gratitude for the blessings in my life. First and foremost, I’m thankful for you and this incredible community of supporters who make our work possible. I’m also grateful for the farmers and growers in our community whose hard work has made it possible for so many of us to put food on our tables today. And I'm incredibly grateful to represent Oregon’s 6th District. It's the honor of a lifetime, and your faith in me is what allows us to keep fighting for the issues that matter most. Together, we’ve taken steps to lower costs, protect abortion rights, and build a fairer economy – and I know that together, we will keep up this fight in the coming months and years. While this work is not easy, this season of gratitude reminds me why we can’t give up: to keep the American Dream alive for families like mine and yours. Thank you for being part of this journey and Happy Thanksgiving. Your support means everything to me, and I hope your day is filled with love, laughter, and maybe a second helping of pie. Thanks, Andrea Salinas

---

name: Rev. Darrius Butler
subject: happy Thanksgiving
body:
Very few times in our lives do we have the chance to so clearly see our effect on the people around us, to hear their voices, and even gain their support and trust. Happy Thanksgiving, friend.

This year, the holidays may be a tense time for families across the nation.

Following a historic upset of an election, dinner table conversation will turn to politics and the direction our country will be taking in the next four years.

**As a candidate for office, I have a particular vantage point on the election.**

Over 100,000 people voted for me, placing their trust in me and my campaign this election, a total that’s both honoring and a bit staggering.

Very few times in our lives do we have the chance to so clearly see our effect on the people around us, to hear their voices, and even gain their support and trust. I’ve been blessed to receive that opportunity this year, and I want to stay in gratitude and awe of that experience.

**But believe me — given the results across the country it would be easy to complain around my Thanksgiving table this year.**

It’s important that I remember that it truly is an incredibly rare and precious opportunity to run for office. But running for office is just one piece of the puzzle.

Democracy is a delicate thing, and despite the results, I’m so proud that Georgians turned out to protect their rights and use their vote — it’s the only way we keep both.

Remember that you have power. Not just with your vote, but with every action you take in your community. It’s up to us to use those moments to make our corner of the world better. And I’m thankful for the opportunity that gives me every day.

Wishing you and your families a peaceful and bright Thanksgiving,
— Rev. Darrius Butler

* * *
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/97810090/514659473/-330727681?refcode=footerlogo&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjEvMS8xMTI0OTciLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMGU4ZWNhYjQtYTlhZC1lZjExLTg4Y2YtNjA0NWJkZmU4ZDI5IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=tsdBmLQXnmlgGWEFtbpzShXWO27ePdt2CaTriHJwSSA=)
Raised by public servants and pastors, Rev. Darrius Butler is a minister and nonprofit leader dedicated to helping Georgians in need. Darrius knows that the families in his district deserve someone who gives a damn.

If you believe you received this email in error or would no longer like to receive email updates from the campaign, please click here to **_[unsubscribe](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/vIqp_9LXQU22XgQprujthA2?unsubscribedata=MirrIrm8SXYeGwA%2FDKKHfSQQiM94CTrCv%2FwWZ2h%2BB6YXPFk%2BjWPk%2BlQ5g8sd5twDSrdxkd55gpc4EQF3%2F7yELlRPLlLkSS999JqlkVQ5EW%2FsIYrGNkcyPvB4IdAkPw3N9lmSIImLhp8Y%2FnKRuDcQiVE2P5C%2FuBg9BMURC5AZJ777dabytcDe6idjFFk1zDApglg8gtl8nENUnwjwFj8QftG1bbYlATWsdUDrumjfQGwej4m%2FP0Y92LHynqE3I7vYCD60TgrWc0nXm45vHwafXr8CR26LkWdjjm%2FfZ0LBRhrCYFO%2FmyySYVa%2By6Q4F8E94c2e4YTE6dqIbhUzY4vhk9Ysf0PgpPyF0FU%2F0VURy%2B3ue%2F8ujNXjHn4Z3O4dLcWyM68uP93bbYuRIKxXNxDMBmJmwEnPB%2FMWyY0%2ByJpb1zD7ihwBVQrRebfR9FTAK2Cg)._**
